source.title: probabilistic-alert-smoothing-eval-gate: add stochastic CI gate to reduce noisy prompt-regression alerts
source.author: Asha Patel
source.description: Motivation: CI has been producing a high volume of transient prompt-regression alerts for low-volume cohorts. These noisy failures increase on-call toil and delay prioritization of true regressions. This change introduces a probabilistic/stochastic gating layer that smooths alerts by estimating a posterior over the cohort regression rate and only opening an incident when the posterior crosses a configurable credibility threshold. Design: - New CI hook `prob-gate` computes a Beta posterior over passed/failed sample counts per cohort, with configurable prior (alpha/beta) and lookback window. - Gate emits three outcomes: allow-fail (treat as transient), gated-fail (record but do not page), and open-incident (page and block merge). - A lightweight failover path triggers a full evaluation run for cohorts that are near-threshold to reduce false positives. - Integrates with existing eval-harness reporters and the CI summary step; metrics emitted for telemetry (posterior_mean, posterior_ci_low, posterior_ci_high, samples_considered). Backwards compatibility: Default configuration preserves current deterministic threshold behavior (alpha=1,beta=1,credibility=0.99) so rollout is opt-in. Configuration is exposed via ci/eval-hooks.yml and via environment variables for Dedicated/Private runs. Implementation notes: - Core logic implemented in eval_harness/hooks/probabilistic_gate.py - CI integration and annotations in ci/hooks/prob-gate.yml - Unit tests in tests/test_probabilistic_gate.py and an integration test fixture using a small synthetic prompt cohort to validate smoothing behavior - Docs in docs/dev/eval-gates.md and example config in examples/ci/prob-gate-example.yaml Checklist: - [x] Core algorithm implemented - [x] Unit + integration tests - [x] CI config and annotations - [x] Docs + example config - [x] Telemetry counters and dashboards (PR links included) Impact: This should reduce alert noise for low-sample cohorts while preserving sensitivity for real regressions. Teams can tune alpha/beta/credibility to match risk tolerance. See release notes for how to opt-in and suggested defaults for evaluation suites.

source.release_notes: Adds a probabilistic CI gate to smooth low-sample prompt-regression alerts. Opt-in via ci/hooks/prob-gate.yml. Default behavior preserves existing thresholding. Suggested defaults and tuning guidance included in docs/dev/eval-gates.md.
review_comments: Priya Natarajan: Can we expose the prior (alpha/beta) as an env var so teams can tune without editing YAML?
Asha Patel: Yes, added ENV knobs PROB_GATE_ALPHA/PROB_GATE_BETA and updated docs with examples (see examples/ci/prob-gate-example.yaml).
Miguel Santos: CI flaked on the integration test on first run (timeout on synthetic fixture). Recommend adding deterministic seeding to tests.
Asha Patel: Added deterministic seed to the fixture and reduced the synthetic cohort size to keep CI runtime under the timeout. Reran tests locally and in CI.
Ethan Caldwell: Approving after addressing telemetry naming nit and adding a comment to explain the credibility threshold math.
CI Bot: pipeline/qa: failed on first run (integration timeout), rerun triggered by author.
CI Bot: pipeline/qa: pass on second run. All checks green.

source.title: add difficulty-calibrator-and-heuristic-resampler-ci-hook
source.author: Priya Rao
source.release_notes: Adds a prompt difficulty calibration module and a CI gate that detects and rejects regressions attributable to shifts in prompt difficulty. Includes a heuristic resampler to rebalance prompt cohorts and a docs page describing knob settings and rollout guidance.
source.description: Motivation: CI quality gates have been vulnerable to false positives/negatives when prompt sets shift subtly in difficulty (length, novelty vs. anchor set, generation entropy). This PR introduces a difficulty-calibrator component that scores prompts by a composite difficulty metric and a heuristic resampler used both during local eval runs and as a CI hook to detect distributional shifts. What changed: 1) eval_harness/difficulty/calibrator.py: new module that computes a composite difficulty score per prompt (token-length-normalized surprisal proxy from a lightweight LM scoring pass, embedding distance to anchored prompt centroids, and response-model entropy estimate). 2) eval_harness/ci/hooks/difficulty_gate.py: CI hook that compares the baseline difficulty distribution (stored as a short histogram blob in CI artifacts) to the current run using a K-S style test plus a shift-aware regression control. 3) eval_harness/samplers/heuristic_resampler.py: on-disk reweighting/resampling strategy to rebalance evaluation batches by difficulty quintiles and optionally prioritize historically unstable prompts. 4) tests/*: unit tests and small integration smoke tests. 5) docs/eval/difficulty_calibrator.md: design doc and recommended thresholds for CI. Design notes / how it works: - Composite difficulty score is a weighted sum (configurable) of:  a) normalized surprisal estimate (fast model-forward on a small ngram LM checkpoint)  b) semantic novelty (embedding cosine distance to nearest 50-anchor prompts)  c) response entropy proxy derived from prewired model-forward token logits for a short rollout. - The CI hook computes two signals: (A) distributional shift score (K-S on difficulty scalar) and (B) difficulty-conditioned regression delta: prompts are bucketed into quintiles and the primary quality metric (e.g., F1/ROUGE/semantic-sim) is compared per-bin to baseline; the hook fails only if either (1) the overall shift exceeds a tunable threshold AND the difficulty-conditioned per-bin regression exceeds per-bin thresholds OR (2) there is a significant regression concentrated in a bin with low sample count that cannot be explained by sampling variance. - The heuristic resampler reweights the eval queue to match a target difficulty distribution (default: baseline quintile proportions) and includes an 'attenuate-hotspots' option to avoid repeatedly exposing the same unstable prompts. Testing & CI: - Added deterministic-seed tests for calibrator computations and a small simulated-run integration test that exercises the gate with contrived baseline/current distributions. - CI initially failed on test_rebalance_sample due to non-determinism in the random seed used by the sampling smoke test; fixed by pinning RNG in the test and re-running. - Full pipeline (unit + smoke + lint) passed on rerun. Rollout plan: - Enabled as opt-in in CI for 1 week (flag: ci.enable_difficulty_gate=false by default). After observing false positive rate <1% on main branch historical windows and one week of canary on the release/* lanes, flip to opt-out. - Ops/Oncall runbook section added in docs describing how to interpret per-bin regressions and how to backfill baseline histograms. Backwards compatibility: default behavior is no-op unless baseline artifact present and ci flag enabled; no breaking changes to public APIs.

small_diff_snippets:
+++ eval_harness/difficulty/calibrator.py
+def score_prompt(prompt, anchors, lm, embedder, cfg):
+    # fast surprisal approx + embedding novelty + entropy proxy
+    s = lm.fast_surprisal(prompt) / max(1, len(prompt.split()))
+    e = 1 - max_cosine_similarity(embedder.embed(prompt), anchors)
+    h = lm.response_entropy_proxy(prompt, rollout=cfg.rollout_steps)
+    return cfg.alpha * s + cfg.beta * e + cfg.gamma * h
review_comments: Ethan Park: Nice approach. A few questions: 1) how sensitive is the composite score to the LM used for surprisal? We should document the recommended small LM and provide a fallback. 2) Can we avoid storing full histograms in CI artifacts (size concerns)? Consider bucketing to quintiles / small sketch.

Priya Rao (author): Answered — surprisal uses a small distilled 125M LM by default; configuration allows swapping to a larger model if teams prefer higher fidelity. Histograms are stored as 5-quintile counts by default to cap size; full-precision debug blobs are kept only when ci.debug=true.
Marta Gonzalez: Request change — add a deterministic seed to the resampler smoke test. Also, please add a short note in docs on how threshold values were selected (calibration run).
Priya Rao: Addressed — pinned RNG in tests and added calibration note under 'Selecting thresholds' in docs/eval/difficulty_calibrator.md. Updated tests and docs in follow-up commit.
Samuel Lee: Approve after comments addressed. Concern: ops runbook should include a quick mitigation (e.g., temporarily disable gate) — please add this to docs.
Priya Rao: Added runbook mitigation steps in docs and included the ci flag for rapid disable.
ci_log_summary: Run #1: unit tests + lint — lint passed, unit tests failed (1 flaky resampling test) — status: failed
Action: pinned RNG and re-ran failing test locally; pushed fix
Run #2: full pipeline — unit + smoke + lint — all passed; difficulty_gate executed in experimental matrix and produced expected no-op results without baseline artifact
source.merge_outcome: Merged to main via squash after reviewers approved. No rollback needed. Opt-in CI hook added to experimental matrix; docs updated with runbook and calibration notes.
source.notes_for_oncall: If a quality CI failure is labeled 'difficulty-shifted-quality-regression', check the per-quintile delta report attached to the CI artifact. If regressions are concentrated in high-difficulty quintiles, consider: 1) re-running evaluations with the heuristic_resampler to increase sample size, 2) temporarily disabling the gate by setting ci.enable_difficulty_gate=false in the CI config, 3) filing SUP ticket with dataset and run ids to request a manual triage. Baseline histograms can be backfilled using 'eval_harness/tools/backfill_baseline --from-run <run-id>'.

source.title: Longtail SLO meter and annotation adapter
source.author: Priya Nandakumar
source.release_notes: Adds a long-tail SLO meter that aggregates low-frequency failure modes into a decay-weighted signal, an annotation adapter to attach contextual evidence to flagged prompt-sets, and a CI soft-fail integration that converts sustained SLO deviation into an actionable Linear ticket.
source.description: Motivation: We have seen sporadic, low-volume prompt-set failures that escape per-run thresholds but aggregate into customer-visible issues over time (long tail). This PR implements a Longtail SLO Meter that maintains a decay-weighted time series of failure signals and a companion Annotation Adapter to attach contextual evidence (examples, model variant, env) to flagged prompt-sets. The goal is to surface gradual SLO drift earlier and enable actionable handoff to triage workflows without increasing noisy CI failures. Design summary: 1) longtail_meter module: computes an exponentially decayed aggregate for each prompt-cohort keyed by prompt_set_id + model_variant, supports configurable half-life and bucket lengths; 2) annotation_adapter: lightweight pluggable interface (sync+async) that writes structured evidence to eval metadata and an optional annotations store; 3) ci-integration: a CI soft-fail mode that transitions from local eval failure -> sustained longtail deviation -> automatic Linear ticket creation with pre-filled evidence. Backwards compatibility: default behavior is opt-in via eval config. No changes to existing eval schema unless the annotation_adapter is enabled. Config knobs: decay_half_life (minutes/hours/days), min_sample_threshold, sustained_window_count, alert_threshold. Testing: unit tests for aggregator math, integration tests with synthetic prompt-sets, end-to-end smoke run against nightly eval harness. Rollout plan: gated behind feature flag and gradual enabling for internal teams before rolling to public CI gating. Related work: ENG-4287 (slo instrumentation), ENG-4301 (annotation store RFC).

source.review_thread:
Diego Morales (reviewer): Can you explain how decay_half_life maps to bucket size? Priya: The half-life controls the exponential weight; bucket_size is orthogonal and controls aggregation granularity. We expose both knobs but recommend a default 24h half-life with 1h buckets. Diego: Add a short doc note with recommended defaults. ACTION taken.
Ava Chen: Concern about creating too many Linear tickets from noisy signals. Proposed: require sustained_window_count >= 3 and min_sample_threshold >= 50 by default. Priya: Implemented thresholding and added jitter suppression in the ci hook.
Samuel O'Neill: Suggests optimistic path for annotation_adapter: avoid blocking eval runs on remote adapter failures. Priya: added async background writer with bounded retry and local cache fallback.
Diego: Minor style nits and type hints requested. Priya: addressed in follow-up commit.
Final: approvals from Diego and Ava; Samuel gave comment + thumbs-up after background writer change.
source.ci_summary: CI run: majority of checks green. Flaky test tests/integration/test_longtail_end2end.py failed once on first run due to fixture timeout; re-run succeeded. Lint and type checks passed. Performance microbenchmark shows <1.5% overhead on median eval latency in local smoke; added a warning if decay half-life configured extremely small. All checks passing at merge time.
source.merge_result: Merged via squash on 2026-03-03 by Priya Nandakumar. Commit message consolidated design rationale and key knobs. Post-merge tasks: create onboarding doc for internal teams, add feature-flag auditing in the console, and schedule a cross-team demo. Linear ticket auto-creation path tested end-to-end with sandbox token and a dry-run mode enabled by default.
source.notes: Operational considerations: annotation storage is opt-in to avoid PII capture; adapter includes a redact hook. The longtail meter exposes Prometheus metrics (longtail_decay_value, longtail_samples_count, longtail_alerts_created) and tracing for sample ingestion. Future work: sampled evidence attachments to traces, adaptive half-life tuning based on traffic seasonality.

source.title: Contextual weighted sample allocator and gradual restrictor for stable eval gating
source.author: Marina Lopes
source.release_notes: Adds a contextual weighted sampling allocator for eval harnesses and a gradual restrictor to soften CI hard-blocking. Improves regression gating stability by blending recent drift signals with prompt-level importance weights. No user-facing API changes; affects eval orchestration and CI decisioning.
source.description:
Motivation: CI evals have been noisy for lower-volume prompt cohorts. Teams are getting frequent false positives when a small cohort flips. This PR implements a contextual weighted sample allocator that allocates probe budget across prompt sets using a combination of: (1) prompt-level importance (manually tagged or derived from production hit rates), (2) recent drift score (rolling 7-day), and (3) semantic novelty (via embedding distance).

Design: The allocator produces proportional sample quotas per cohort and emits a per-run sampling plan. A complementary GradualRestrictor replaces the previous immediate hard-blocking CI hook: instead of a binary fail, we compute a severity score and apply a staged response (soft-fail annotations, delayed blocking windows, and adaptive replays). This reduces churn while preserving safety for true regressions.

What changed (high level):
- New allocator: eval_harness/sampler/contextual_allocator.py (core logic, pluggable backends)
- Gradual restrictor: eval_harness/ci/gradual_restrictor.py (CI hook adapter)
- Scheduler integration: eval_harness/orchestrator/sampling_scheduler.py (wiring + metrics)
- Tests: tests/samplers/test_contextual_allocator.py, tests/ci/test_gradual_restrictor.py
- Docs: docs/evals/sampling.md (usage + config knobs)

Compatibility and config: The allocator is off by default. Config keys under eval.sampling.contextual_enabled=true to enable. Backwards-compatible: if not enabled the legacy uniform sampler is used. title: Contextual weighted sample allocator and gradual restrictor for stable eval gating
preview:
Motivation: CI evals have been noisy for lower-volume prompt cohorts. Teams are getting frequent false positives when a small cohort flips. This PR implements a contextual weighted sample allocator that allocates probe budget across prompt sets using a combination of: (1) prompt-level importance (manually tagged or derived from production hit rates), (2) recent drift score (rolling 7-day), and (3) semantic novelty (via embedding distance).

Design: The allocator produces proportional sample quotas per cohort and emits a per-run sampling plan. A complementary GradualRestrictor replaces the previous immediate hard-blocking CI hook: instead of a binary fail, we compute a severity score and apply a staged response (soft-fail annotations, delayed blocking windows, and adaptive replays). This reduces churn while preserving safety for true regressions.

source.title: Introduce hierarchical fidelity oracle and non-blocking CI adapter
source.author: Maya Liu
source.description: Motivation: existing CI gates are brittle for prompt sets with heterogeneous lengths, multi-turn contexts and low-frequency anchors which lead to noisy false positives. This PR introduces a hierarchical fidelity oracle that fuses multiple evaluation signals (semantic similarity, role-aware turn consistency, token-level saliency, and anchor stability) across temporal cohorts and prompt granularity, and exposes a non-blocking CI adapter that surfaces soft-fail quality regressions with prioritized remediation suggestions. Core behaviors: 1) compute per-prompt multi-signal fidelity vectors, 2) aggregate per-cohort with recency-aware weighting and uncertainty calibration, 3) emit composite fidelity score and explainability hints (top contributing tokens and failing turns), 4) provide a non-blocking CI decision path which fails only on high-confidence regressions and otherwise annotates PRs with risk tiers and suggested rerun probes. This change contains: implementation of scorer and adapter, integration tests, docs and an example anchor manifest. Checklist: [x] implement hierarchical_fidelity scorer; [x] adapter for CI (non-blocking by default, configurable thresholds); [x] integration tests with synthetic perturbation reruns; [x] docs and example anchor prompt set; [x] Linear links (ENG-4523, ENG-6110) added to PR. Testing notes: local unit and integration tests added; synthetic rerun harness simulates paraphrase/negation/noise and verifies attenuation of false positives vs current thresholded gate; metrics show 22% reduction in spurious CI failures on internal smoke corpus. Rollout plan: gate is opt-in per-repo via a small YAML config in .eval/config.yaml, default mode is "annotate-only"; after two weeks of opt-in telemetry, global adoption can be proposed. Backwards compatibility: adapter is opt-in and does not change existing blocking gates. Follow-ups: add cross-model calibration (ENG-6110) and dashboarding to Console for fidelity deltas.

source.title: Introduce influence-matrix eval hook and rollback scorer
source.author: Elena Vasquez
source.description:
Motivation: We've had recurring incidents where a small prompt tweak in a PR produced outsized regressions for a narrow but critical slice of prompt-sets. Existing CI smokechecks flag macro regressions but provide little signal about which prompts, tokens, or candidate edits are most influential. This PR adds an 'influence matrix' eval hook that attributes per-prompt and per-perturbation influence on aggregate metrics, and a lightweight rollback scorer that suggests the smallest prompt change(s) that would return metrics to baseline.

What this does: Implements an eval hook that runs alongside existing regression checks. For each prompt-set it computes an influence score per prompt and per change vector using a randomized leave-k-out approximation (bounded sampling plus importance-weighting) and aggregates into an influence matrix (prompt x perturbation). The CI adapter consumes aggregated scores and will soft-block PRs that exceed a configurable impact threshold, surface a ranked list of top-k harmful prompts in the PR UI, and attach a rollback_scorer report with candidate minimal edits (text span revert or constraint relaxations) with estimated delta impact.

Design notes: The algorithm favors speed and interpretability over exact Shapley values; we approximate contribution via stratified sampling and an anchored baseline. Influence values are normalized per-metric and can be weighted by metric severity (e.g., hallucination > BLEU-like). The rollback scorer uses greedy local search over candidate revert spans and prompt parameter toggles (system message, temperature hints) to propose edits with confidence bands.

Backward compatibility: Hook is opt-in per-eval-job via job config and defaults to disabled for long-running production backfills. When enabled it adds a bounded extra eval cost (~10-30% depending on cohort size). The hook exposes tunables: sample_budget, top_k_reports, soft_block_threshold, metric_weights.

Files changed: See files_changed field for a concise list.

Related: ENG-2319 (detect high-impact prompt regressions ahead of rollout), PM-412 (UI work to display ranked prompt influence in PR).

Asha: "This is a great direction—two questions: can we bound worst-case CI time, and do we risk noisy hints for low-traffic prompts?" Elena: "Bounded sampling with sample_budget defaults to 200 per-cohort; we've added an early-exit if variance is under threshold to save cycles. Low-traffic prompts are de-emphasized via metric_weights and an exposure floor so they don't dominate."
Marco: "Can the rollback_scorer propose edits that violate safety policies?" Elena: "Rollback candidates are restricted to reverting changes present in the PR diff or toggling non-content parameters; we also run the existing safety linter over proposed edits and drop any candidate that fails."
Priya: "Will this integrate with the console UI for 'top harmful prompts'?" Elena: "Yes, report schema was coordinated with PM-412; each entry includes score, sample context, and suggested edit."

source.title: Introduce contextual cohort synthesis gate with adaptive weighting
source.author: Priya Shah
release_notes: Add new CI gate: contextual cohort synthesis and adaptive signal weighting
source.description:
Motivation: We frequently see spurious regression signals when prompt distributions shift subtly (new metadata, truncated contexts, or injected system instructions). This PR introduces a CI/managed-gate that synthesizes cohorts at evaluation time based on semantic context clusters and adaptively reweights alert signals so short-term distribution shifts do not produce noisy failures.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a new gate type 'contextual_cohort_synthesis' that can be plugged into the CI eval pipeline; 2) Implements a lightweight vector-backed cohort builder that groups examples by a configurable semantic window (prompt+metadata embedding); 3) Computes weighted regression scores across synthesized cohorts with an attenuation factor tied to cohort volatility; 4) Exposes config knobs (min_cohort_size, volatility_smoothing, fallback_anchor) and a canary rollout policy; 5) Emits detailed telemetry (per-cohort counts, volatility, score_delta, attenuation_applied) to the eval metrics pipeline for downstream dashboards and alerting.

Checklist: - [x] Implementation with unit tests and integration smoke tests; - [x] CI hook integration and example workflow; - [x] Telemetry and dashboard dashboard-ready metrics names; - [x] Documentation snippet for ops and config; - [x] Backfill script to re-run recent failing prompt-sets through the gate.

Usage: The gate is opt-in via eval.yml: gates:
  - type: contextual_cohort_synthesis
    config:
      enabled: true
      min_cohort_size: 8
      volatility_smoothing: 0.6
      fallback_anchor: historical_baseline
      attenuation_threshold: 0.15

Testing notes: Unit tests cover the cohort builder, volatility calculation and attenuation rules. Integration tests run on a small synthetic prompt-set fixture (tests/fixtures/semantic-shift/*) and CI includes a smoke run against a clipped production sample. Local dev: ./tools/eval_runner --gate contextual_cohort_synthesis --sample tests/fixtures/semantic-shift/sample.json.

Risk & fallback: When enabled without an anchor, the attenuation can hide true regressions; default is disabled and CI templates include explicit opt-in. Canary rollout uses a small-sample flag and auto-fallback to the historical baseline gate when cohort volatility exceeds 0.9.

Related: ENG-4321 (contextual gating), PM-1189 (reduce false positive regression alerts), SUP-2210 (customer reported noisy CI alerts).

Integration tests run on a small synthetic prompt-set fixture (tests/fixtures/semantic-shift/*) and CI includes a smoke run against a clipped production sample. Local dev: ./tools/eval_runner --gate contextual_cohort_synthesis --sample tests/fixtures/semantic-shift/sample.json.

Risk & fallback: When enabled without an anchor, the attenuation can hide true regressions; default is disabled and CI templates include explicit opt-in. Canary rollout uses a small-sample flag and auto-fallback to the historical baseline gate when cohort volatility exceeds 0.9.

Related: ENG-4321 (contextual gating), PM-1189 (reduce false positive regression alerts), SUP-2210 (customer reported noisy CI alerts).

source.title: Add regression blame and diff-summary CI check for prompt-set evaluations
source.author: Asha Patel
source.description: Motivation: CI currently reports failing prompt-set metrics but gives limited context to quickly identify the source of a regression. This PR introduces a two-part CI hook: (1) regression-blame which correlates failing prompt cohorts to recent changes in prompt definitions, eval configs, and model pinning; (2) diff-summary which renders a compact human-readable summary of model-output changes for affected prompts. The goal is to reduce mean time-to-diagnose for eval regressions and to make gating decisions actionable. Implementation notes: regression-blame walks the prompt-set git history and the eval config commits in the last 30 days to produce candidate commit ranges; it then scores candidates by overlap with changed prompt IDs, changed tokenization metadata, and run-time seed/temperature differences. diff-summary runs a lightweight paired-output comparator that highlights token-level deltas, normalized scores, and a short semantic-change heuristic (BLEU/ROUGE delta + embedding cosine delta threshold). The CI hook plugs into existing eval GH actions and produces an artifact JSON and a Markdown summary comment. Checklist: [x] core algorithm and scoring, [x] GH action integration, [x] unit tests for scoring heuristics, [x] docs + runbook snippet, [x] small-scale perf bench on large prompt sets (< 10s per 100 prompts on CI runner). Related: Linear ENG-1882, ENG-2001. Jira: SUP-433 (customer report that prompted this work). Usage: when a gated eval fails, the CI will post a comment with a compact diff-summary and a ranked list of probable commits to inspect. The comment links to the full JSON artifact and a generated local html for quick triage. Notes for reviewers: the scoring weights are configurable via eval-config.yaml; defaults are conservative to avoid noisy blame; thresholds are documented. Rollout: implemented behind a feature flag eval_ci_blame enabled for 2 teams initially. This description contains implementation rationale, test plan, and rollout guidance in one continuous string.

d12a6e1  GH action: add eval-regression-blame workflow (runs on eval failure)
e8a7c3b  Add diff-summary renderer + markdown reporter
f02b44d  Docs: ci-eval-hooks.md and runbook snippets
a1d3e90  Tests: end-to-end CI integration test with mocked prompt git history
review_conversation: Marcus Lin (2026-02-21): Nice approach. Two asks: make the scoring weights configurable from eval-config and add an opt-out parameter for very long prompt histories.
Asha Patel (2026-02-21): Done — added eval_config.regression_blame.{weights,max_history_days,opt_out} and updated the README. Also added a safety early-exit when history > max_history_days.
Priya Desai (2026-02-22): Tests look good. One nit: prefer shorter filenames for the artifacts. Suggested renaming sample json to regression-blame-artifact.json.j2.

Asha Patel (2026-02-23): Fixed docs link; ready to merge. CI green after re-run.

source.release_notes: Add CI hook that posts a ranked 'regression blame' and concise diff-summary when eval gating fails, improving triage speed for prompt regressions.
source.guidance_snippet: Interpreting the blame scores: higher score => higher overlap of changed prompt IDs and config diffs; use the diff-summary to validate semantic change before reverting commits.
source.notes_for_ops: Feature gated behind eval_ci_blame; enable for a subset of repos first and monitor CI artifact size; storage retention set to 7 days by default.

source.title: conditional eval fanout + weighted signal aggregator and CI gating
source.author: Ava Chen
source.description: Motivation: when a primary prompt-set check trips, we often need to run a set of targeted probes (e.g. hallucination prober, paraphrase-stability, token-saliency) to determine failure mode before blocking rollouts. This PR adds a conditional eval fanout layer and a weighted signal aggregator so CI can produce a single composite gating decision that reflects both severity and breadth of regressions. Design summary: - New runtime component eval_fanout orchestrates conditional parallel probes with a budget cap and backpressure to avoid blowing smoke-test windows. - WeightedSignalAggregator implements decay-weighted aggregation across cohorts and probes; weights are configurable per-linear-task and default to severity-first then coverage. - CiHook composite_slo_check integrates into existing eval CI: primary prompt-set runs as usual; on fail, fanout triggers configured probes; aggregator emits a human-readable breakdown and a single pass/fail decision. - Added JSON schema for fanout plan (configs/ : fanout-plans/*.json) and migration docs. Changes (high level): - eval/engine/fanout.py (core orchestration + retry/backoff) - eval/metrics/aggregator.py (aggregation logic, normalization) - eval/ci/composite_slo_hook.py (CI entrypoint + reporter) - configs/fanout-plans/english-and-multilingual.json - tests/{unit,integration}/test_fanout_* and smoke scenarios - docs/eval/fanout-design.md (motivation and configuration guide) Checklist: - [x] Unit tests covering core aggregator math and scheduling heuristics - [x] Integration smoke that runs fanout on a small sample prompt set within CI budget - [x] CI gating rule added and tested in staging - [x] Backwards-compatible configs (existing eval runs unchanged unless plan specified) Rollout plan: merge to main, enable composite_slo_check behind feature-flag per-repo, monitor eval/telemetry.composite_gate.* metrics for two weeks, then flip default for canaries. Related: ENG-2048 for production tuning, ENG-2110 for policy UI knobs. Notes: kept probes as light-weight callable adapters so adding new probes doesn't require changing orchestration core. See internal doc: https://linear.app/redwood/board/ENG-2048/fanout-spec for full design.
commits: a1f4c9b: core fanout orchestrator and basic budget/backpressure
b2e8d30: weighted aggregator + normalization and schema validators
c3d4f12: CI hook + reporter + smoke tests + docs
source.commit_summary: Core orchestration, aggregator math, CI hook, probes and tests split across three commits. Key behavior: fanout only triggers on primary check fail and will stop early when aggregator reaches decisive threshold; probes run with per-probe concurrency limits to protect CI budget.
review_comments: Marcus Li (review): overall looks solid. Question: how do we ensure fanout probes cannot cause flakiness by increasing non-determinism in CI? Please add deterministic seeding for probes in integration runs.
Ava Chen (author): added deterministic seeding in tests and included a note in fanout-design.md; also added a smoke integration with fixed RNG seeds.
Hannah Ortiz (review): can we expose the weight config via the console UI later? For now, ensure the defaults are conservative. Also requested clearer error codes from aggregator.
Ava Chen (author): weights are configurable via configs/fanout-plans/*.json; added a small config validation with helpful error messages. Filed follow-up ENG-2110 to add UI surface.
Diego Ramos (approval): minor nits addressed, approving. Recommend monitoring composite_gate.false_positive_rate metric after rollout.
ci_checks: integration-eval: failed on first run (timeout on paraphrase probe), rerun -> pass
source.release_notes: Adds conditional evaluation fanout and a weighted signal aggregator to enable composite CI gating across prompt-set regressions. Enables configurable probe fanout plans and adds an opt-in CI hook 'composite_slo_check'. Monitor composite_gate.* telemetry after rollout.

source.title: Lightweight quality spot-check hooks for perf suite runs
source.author: Jonas Weber
source.release_notes: Adds an optional, low-overhead quality spot-check phase for perf runs (sampling prompts + sanity checks). Intended to catch decoding/config regressions without turning perf benchmarking into a full eval job.
source.body:
Summary: Perf regressions are easy to detect, but we’ve had a few near-misses where perf-focused changes accidentally altered decoding behavior (e.g., defaults drift, chat template mismatch, tool-call formatting toggled, or truncation due to max_tokens changes). This PR adds an optional, lightweight “quality spot-check” hook to run at the end of a perf run, sampling a small prompt set and applying basic sanity checks. It is not intended to be a full quality gate; it is intended to catch obvious breakages early and provide artifacts for triage.

Motivation / context: As part of the Q1 2026 “Perf baseline & representative workload suite” work, we’re standardizing perf runs and integrating canaries/gating. Applied ML asked for minimal guardrails so that we don’t ship a perf win that is actually a decoding/config regression. This PR implements the minimal spot-check plan aligned with the internal memo “perf-vs-quality-guardrails-q1-2026” and is designed to be safe to run in CI and on benchmark fleets.

What’s included: (1) A new optional spot-check phase: `eval-harness spotcheck` can be invoked standalone, or via the existing perf runner hook (`--quality-spotcheck`). The spot-check runs after perf metrics collection by default to avoid perturbing latency/throughput measurements. (2) Prompt set + sampling: Adds `prompts/spotcheck/v1/` with a small, synthetic + hand-curated “must-not-break” set (chat + completion + tool-call shaped prompts). Sampling is deterministic based on `--spotcheck-seed` (default ties to the perf run seed if present). No production prompt content is included. (3) Sanity checks (fast heuristics): non-empty output; UTF-8 decodes; no immediate server-side error indicators; maximum repetition guard (token-level repetition ratio threshold) to catch “stuck loop” regressions; finish_reason presence for supported backends; optional JSON parse check when the prompt declares `expect_json=true`; optional tool-call structure check when the prompt declares `expect_tool_call=true` (checks that the model returns a structured tool call payload vs plain text for our tool-call adapter).

Interfaces / CLI: New flags: `--quality-spotcheck` (bool), `--spotcheck-prompts prompts/spotcheck/v1/prompts.jsonl`, `--spotcheck-sample-size 20` (default 20), `--spotcheck-seed`, `--spotcheck-concurrency 2` (default 2), `--spotcheck-fail-mode warn|hard` (default warn), `--spotcheck-timeout-ms 60000`. Environment: `EVAL_HARNESS_SPOTCHECK=1` enables spot-check in environments where wiring flags is annoying (e.g., some perf-canary configs). Exit codes: `warn` mode always exits 0 but emits a summary and marks failing cases in the artifact; `hard` mode exits non-zero if any prompt fails sanity checks.

Artifacts: Spot-check writes a small JSONL artifact alongside the perf artifacts (for dashboard drill-down and to attach to run manifests). Each line is a single case with: prompt_id, mode (chat/completion), model, region (if present in env), seed, decoding_params hash, ok, checks_failed, finish_reason, output_len_chars, output_len_tokens (when tokenizer available), latency_ms, and a truncated output preview (first ~300 chars) for quick triage. Example snippet (illustrative): `{ "prompt_id": "tool_call_weather_003", "ok": false, "checks_failed": "tool_call_missing;finish_reason_missing", "latency_ms": 412, "output_preview": "Sure—here’s the weather..." }`.

Implementation details: Introduces `SpotcheckRunner` with a narrow interface so benchmark-lab/perf-canary can call it without pulling in perf measurement code paths. Sampling is stable: we shuffle prompt IDs with a seeded RNG and take the first N; prompts can be filtered by mode. Tokenization is optional (if the backend exposes tokenizer info we use it; otherwise repetition checks fall back to character-gram heuristics). All checks are designed to be O(n) over the output and cheap vs a real eval.

Why it won’t contaminate perf results: By default, spot-check runs after the perf workload completes. It uses a separate client instance with its own request IDs and emits metrics under a different namespace/tag set so dashboards can separate perf measurements vs spot-check runs. If you intentionally want to interleave spot-check with perf (not recommended), you can pass `--spotcheck-phase pre` or `--spotcheck-phase interleave` but both are explicitly marked experimental in docs.

Docs: Adds `docs/spotcheck.md` describing usage, typical failure modes, and how to use the artifacts in triage. Also documents the privacy stance (no prod prompt content; prompts are synthetic/handwritten) and intended scope (“sanity checks, not a quality score”).

Tests: Unit tests for prompt parsing/validation, deterministic sampling, JSON parse expectation, tool-call expectation, and repetition guard logic. Added a small integration test with a mocked backend to ensure warn vs hard exit behavior and artifact emission.

Commits (squash-merged): (1) add spotcheck runner + CLI plumbing, (2) add prompt set v1 + schema/validator, (3) add sanity checks and artifact writer, (4) docs + tests, (5) address review feedback (determinism + fail-mode semantics).

Files / areas changed (high level): `src/spotcheck/runner.py`, `src/spotcheck/checks.py`, `src/spotcheck/prompts.py`, `src/cli.py` (flag wiring), `prompts/spotcheck/v1/prompts.jsonl`, `prompts/spotcheck/v1/schema.json`, `docs/spotcheck.md`, `tests/test_spotcheck_*`.

Review notes / discussion highlights: Claire Dubois: requested that the sampling be explicitly tied to the run manifest seed to support reproducibility across reruns; updated so `--spotcheck-seed` defaults to `RUN_MANIFEST.seed` when present, with an explicit log line. Rina Kobayashi: concerned about spot-check affecting perf runs on shared benchmark fleets; moved default phase to post-perf and added a clear warning + tag separation. Dr. Maya Srinivasan: requested stricter language in docs about what this is and isn’t, and confirmation that prompts are non-sensitive; added privacy note and removed an earlier draft prompt that was too close to a real customer workflow. Mateo Alvarez / Sofia Ivanova: asked that hard failures be opt-in until we have baseline failure rates; set default fail-mode to warn and added guidance for when to flip to hard (release gating).

CI: Initially failed `lint-python` due to unused import in checks module; fixed in follow-up commit. Final checks green: unit tests, lint, typecheck, packaging.

Outcome: Merged (squash) into main. Follow-ups: After this lands, perf-canary will wire `--quality-spotcheck` for nightly runs in warn mode for a calibration period; we’ll propose moving release-branch gating to hard mode once we have a stable baseline failure rate and a small allowlist of known-benign flakes.

What’s included: (1) A new optional spot-check phase: `eval-harness spotcheck` can be invoked standalone, or via the existing perf runner hook (`--quality-spotcheck`). The spot-check runs after perf metrics collection by default to avoid perturbing latency/throughput measurements. (2) Prompt set + sampling: Adds `prompts/spotcheck/v1/` with a small, synthetic + hand-curated “must-not-break” set (chat + completion + tool-call shaped prompts). Sampling is deterministic based on `--spotcheck-seed` (default ties to the perf run seed if present). No production prompt content is included. (3) Sanity checks (fast heuristics): non-empty output; UTF-8 decodes; no immediate server-side error indicators; maximum repetition guard (token-level repetition ratio threshold) to catch “stuck loop” regressions; finish_reason presence for supported backends; optional JSON parse check when the prompt declares `expect_json=true`; optional tool-call structure check when the prompt declares `expect_tool_call=true` (checks that the model returns a structured tool call payload vs plain text for our tool-call adapter).

Interfaces / CLI: New flags: `--quality-spotcheck` (bool), `--spotcheck-prompts prompts/spotcheck/v1/prompts.jsonl`, `--spotcheck-sample-size 20` (default 20), `--spotcheck-seed`, `--spotcheck-concurrency 2` (default 2), `--spotcheck-fail-mode warn|hard` (default warn), `--spotcheck-timeout-ms 60000`. Environment: `EVAL_HARNESS_SPOTCHECK=1` enables spot-check in environments where wiring flags is annoying (e.g., some perf-canary configs). Exit codes: `warn` mode always exits 0 but emits a summary and marks failing cases in the artifact; `hard` mode exits non-zero if any prompt fails sanity checks.

Artifacts: Spot-check writes a small JSONL artifact alongside the perf artifacts (for dashboard drill-down and to attach to run manifests). Each line is a single case with: prompt_id, mode (chat/completion), model, region (if present in env), seed, decoding_params hash, ok, checks_failed, finish_reason, output_len_chars, output_len_tokens (when tokenizer available), latency_ms, and a truncated output preview (first ~300 chars) for quick triage. Example snippet (illustrative): `{ "prompt_id": "tool_call_weather_003", "ok": false, "checks_failed": "tool_call_missing;finish_reason_missing", "latency_ms": 412, "output_preview": "Sure—here’s the weather..." }`.

Implementation details: Introduces `SpotcheckRunner` with a narrow interface so benchmark-lab/perf-canary can call it without pulling in perf measurement code paths. Sampling is stable: we shuffle prompt IDs with a seeded RNG and take the first N; prompts can be filtered by mode. Tokenization is optional (if the backend exposes tokenizer info we use it; otherwise repetition checks fall back to character-gram heuristics). All checks are designed to be O(n) over the output and cheap vs a real eval.

Why it won’t contaminate perf results: By default, spot-check runs after the perf workload completes. It uses a separate client instance with its own request IDs and emits metrics under a different namespace/tag set so dashboards can separate perf measurements vs spot-check runs. If you intentionally want to interleave spot-check with perf (not recommended), you can pass `--spotcheck-phase pre` or `--spotcheck-phase interleave` but both are explicitly marked experimental in docs.

Docs: Adds `docs/spotcheck.md` describing usage, typical failure modes, and how to use the artifacts in triage. Also documents the privacy stance (no prod prompt content; prompts are synthetic/handwritten) and intended scope (“sanity checks, not a quality score”).

Tests: Unit tests for prompt parsing/validation, deterministic sampling, JSON parse expectation, tool-call expectation, and repetition guard logic. Added a small integration test with a mocked backend to ensure warn vs hard exit behavior and artifact emission.

Commits (squash-merged): (1) add spotcheck runner + CLI plumbing, (2) add prompt set v1 + schema/validator, (3) add sanity checks and artifact writer, (4) docs + tests, (5) address review feedback (determinism + fail-mode semantics).

Files / areas changed (high level): `src/spotcheck/runner.py`, `src/spotcheck/checks.py`, `src/spotcheck/prompts.py`, `src/cli.py` (flag wiring), `prompts/spotcheck/v1/prompts.jsonl`, `prompts/spotcheck/v1/schema.json`, `docs/spotcheck.md`, `tests/test_spotcheck_*`.

Review notes / discussion highlights: Claire Dubois: requested that the sampling be explicitly tied to the run manifest seed to support reproducibility across reruns; updated so `--spotcheck-seed` defaults to `RUN_MANIFEST.seed` when present, with an explicit log line. Rina Kobayashi: concerned about spot-check affecting perf runs on shared benchmark fleets; moved default phase to post-perf and added a clear warning + tag separation. Dr. Maya Srinivasan: requested stricter language in docs about what this is and isn’t, and confirmation that prompts are non-sensitive; added privacy note and removed an earlier draft prompt that was too close to a real customer workflow. Mateo Alvarez / Sofia Ivanova: asked that hard failures be opt-in until we have baseline failure rates; set default fail-mode to warn and added guidance for when to flip to hard (release gating).

CI: Initially failed `lint-python` due to unused import in checks module; fixed in follow-up commit. Final checks green: unit tests, lint, typecheck, packaging.

Outcome: Merged (squash) into main. Follow-ups: After this lands, perf-canary will wire `--quality-spotcheck` for nightly runs in warn mode for a calibration period; we’ll propose moving release-branch gating to hard mode once we have a stable baseline failure rate and a small allowlist of known-benign flakes.

source.title: Introduce variant-weighted phased gate and explainable failure reporter
source.author: Rina Kapoor
source.description:
Motivation: We need a gate that can phase-in stricter regression checks across multiple prompt-set variants and weight them by production traffic mix. Existing gates are either binary or treat variants equally, which causes noisy failures from low-impact variants and slow rollouts when a single high-volume variant changes. This PR implements a phased "variant-weighted" quality gate plus an explainable failure reporter that surfaces per-variant contribution and suggested mitigations.

Summary of changes: added a PhaseGate orchestration that accepts variant weights and stage thresholds, a scoring adapter that produces per-variant metrics (accuracy, token-loss, semantic-distance), and an explainability module that generates a short failure report with example prompts, model outputs, and a concise mitigation recommendation. The gate supports three phases (shadow, canary, enforce) and automatically escalates or rolls back based on weighted aggregate score and high-impact-variant signals.

Why this is different: unlike our ensemble or cohort gates this approach natively models traffic-weighted importance, reduces alert fatigue from rare variants, and provides human-readable remediation suggestions to speed incident response. It also integrates with the CI decision buffer to allow staged rollouts during merge checks.

Checklist: - [x] PhaseGate core implementation - [x] Explainable failure reporter - [x] Unit + integration tests - [x] CI hook to evaluate only affected prompt-sets - [x] Telemetry (per-variant metrics and overall weighted score) - [x] Documentation + runbook snippet

Backwards compatibility: The gate is opt-in; existing configuration remains unchanged. Defaults preserve current behavior by uniform weighting and single-phase enforcement.

Operational notes (included in runbook): - The report includes top-5 contributing prompts per-variant and a suggested temporary mitigator (shadow traffic split or pin previous model). - Recommended default weights are derived from a weekly traffic sampler and can be overridden per-linear ticket.

Release notes: add a new "variant-weighted phased quality gate" for eval-harness; provides explainable failure reports and phased rollouts to reduce noisy CI failures and speed triage.

source.title: Introduce per-prompt temporal baseline probe, drift-aware CI gate, and lightweight counterfactual replayer
source.author: Maya Chen
source.description:
Motivation: Some prompt-set regressions we observe are intermittent and tied to recent temporal shifts in training data or model weights. Existing cohort/anchor approaches flag regressions but often require manual baseline selection or suffer from noise when the underlying distribution evolves. This PR adds a Per-Prompt Temporal Baseline (PTB) probe that maintains short- and long-window baselines per prompt, a drift-aware CI gate that reasons about recent baseline movement before failing a job, and a lightweight counterfactual replayer used to prioritize regressions for human review.

Design summary: The PTB probe (eval_harness.probes.ptb_probe) computes exponentially weighted aggregates (EWMA) of configured metrics per prompt across two windows: short (default 1d) and long (default 30d). The probe exposes three signals: absolute delta (short vs long), variance ratio, and a stability score combining token-level variance and aggregated metric delta. The CI gate (ci/hooks/ptb_gate.py) uses these signals to decide fail/soft-fail behavior: if absolute delta exceeds the hard threshold we fail; if delta is between soft and hard thresholds we emit a soft-fail with attached diagnoses and optionally enqueue counterfactual replays.

Counterfactual replayer: A new lightweight replayer (eval_harness.tools.counterfactual_replayer) can replay a small, latency-bounded subset of sampled historical inputs against the PR head model variant and against a pinned stable model to produce a prioritized list of likely regressions. The replayer is intentionally cheap (bounded tokens and sample size) so it can run in CI without blowing budgets. It includes deterministic seeding options for reproducibility.

APIs and config: Introduces a new probe registration entry in probes/registry.yaml with tunable params (short_window, long_window, ewma_alpha, soft_threshold, hard_threshold, replay_budget). CI integration exposes these flags via .github/workflows/eval.yml as optional job inputs. Default gate behavior is non-blocking (soft-fail) for self-serve runs; for release branches the gate is enabled as hard-fail.

Testing and verification: Added unit tests covering EWMA convergence, signal thresholds, and replayer determinism (tests/test_ptb_probe.py). Added an integration smoke job in ci/integration/ptb_smoke.yml that runs the probe on a synthetic prompt set and validates gating behavior. Local benchmarks show the replayer adds ~8% CI runtime under the default replay_budget=50 samples.

Rollout plan: Start with opt-in on main via a feature flag (env PTB_GATE_MODE=soft). After two weeks of telemetry (false-positive rate, queue depth, latency) we plan to flip the default to hard for release branches only and surface auto-created prioritized tickets in Linear for prompts with persistent hard-fail signals.

Migration notes: No breaking changes to existing probe interfaces. Existing registries will pick up the PTB probe by default but the gate remains opt-in. See docs/eval/ptb.md for configuration and troubleshooting.

Related: Linear ENG-1984 (per-prompt stability monitoring), PM-422 (release gate automation). Support ticket SUP-6123 provided customer repro prompting this work.

b9e7c4f: add CI gate hook, workflow inputs, and smoke integration job
d1c3a9e: tests, docs, and counterfactual replayer implementation
review_thread: Ethan Park: Looks good overall. Can you justify the default EWMA alpha choices and where the numbers came from?
Maya Chen: Added comment and unit test demonstrating sensitivity scan; defaults tuned from 7-day synthetic replay experiments (see docs/ptb.md#tuning).

source.title: Add trigger endpoint for Optimize quality gating (config diff + baseline)
source.author: Olivia Grant
source.description: Context: Optimize needs a first-class way to run offline evals against a proposed serving config change (batching/caching/quantization profile) before allowing apply/rollout. This PR adds a new API endpoint and job plumbing in eval-harness to accept a config diff + baseline reference, execute the requested gate tier against selected prompt sets, and return structured metrics plus artifact pointers suitable for Console/Optimize consumption. Endpoint: POST /api/v1/gates/trigger (auth: service-to-service token; scoped to optimize-gating). Request (JSON): gate_tier ("smoke"|"full"), model_id, baseline (baseline_model_version + baseline_runtime_config_ref), proposed (runtime_config_diff, optional proposed_model_version), prompt_sets (ids), sample_count (optional; defaults per tier), run_metadata (route_id, account_id, region, commit_sha, idempotency_key). Validation: limits request payload size, rejects unknown diff keys, enforces that baseline ref is immutable (no "latest"), and checks prompt set ids exist and are enabled for gating. Execution: enqueues a GateRun job to the existing eval runner queue, pins model + runtime settings for the duration of the run, and writes an immutable run record with normalized inputs for audit/debug. Response: 202 Accepted with gate_run_id + status_url + estimated_duration_s; once complete, GET /api/v1/gates/runs/{id} returns status, summary metrics (per prompt set and overall), and artifact pointers (artifact_store_uri + optional signed_url when allowed), including: raw per-example outputs, rubric/score breakdowns, and a compact "comparison.json" (baseline vs proposed deltas). Artifacts: stored under artifact_store_uri prefix gates/{gate_run_id}/..., with retention aligned to existing eval artifact policy; Optimize can deep-link using gate_run_id. Observability: emits gate_trigger_requests_total and gate_run_enqueue_latency_ms (tags: gate_tier, model_id, region, caller=optimize) and attaches trace/span attributes for correlation from Optimize -> eval-harness. Tests: adds contract tests for request validation + schema stability, a fake runner integration test that asserts correct pinning of baseline/proposed configs, and golden JSON snapshots for the run detail response. Notes: This PR does not implement flake scoring/rerun aggregation (tracked in pr-418); response schema includes an optional flake_score field but it is not populated yet. Rollout: endpoint is behind feature flag OPTIMIZE_GATING_TRIGGER_API and requires allowlisted client identity; we can safely deploy without enabling any callers.
commits: api: add gates trigger + run detail endpoints
runner: enqueue GateRun jobs with normalized inputs
storage: write artifact pointers and comparison manifest
tests: contract + snapshot tests for Optimize gating responses
docs: update internal OpenAPI + curl examples
changed_areas: src/core/gates/gate_run_service.ts (persist inputs, enqueue job, status transitions)
src/core/artifacts/artifact_store.ts (gate run artifact prefix + manifest writer)
review_conversation: Jonas Weber: Can we ensure baseline is immutable? We should reject 'latest' and require an explicit version/ref so Optimize can't accidentally compare against a moving target.
Olivia Grant (author): Added baseline_runtime_config_ref validation + explicit version requirement; request now rejects 'latest' and any ref without a content hash.
Selene Huang: Please include per-prompt-set summary in the run detail response; Optimize needs to surface which suite failed, not just overall.
Olivia Grant (author): Updated response schema to include prompt_set_results[] with aggregate metrics + artifact pointer per suite; added snapshot tests.
Connor O'Brien: CI note—these tests will be run in CPU-only jobs. Make sure the fake runner path is deterministic and doesn't require GPU.
Olivia Grant (author): Switched integration test to FakeExecutor; runner job only asserts config pinning + manifest write, no model execution.
Ethan Park: Any reason this isn't idempotent? Optimize will retry on 5xx; we should support idempotency_key to avoid duplicate runs.
Olivia Grant (author): Added idempotency_key to request; service now dedupes within 24h on (caller, idempotency_key) and returns existing gate_run_id.
Jonas Weber: Approving with the caveat that flake_score is optional and must remain nullable until pr-418 lands.
Selene Huang: LGTM after the per-suite summary; thanks.

This PR adds a new API endpoint and job plumbing in eval-harness to accept a config diff + baseline reference, execute the requested gate tier against selected prompt sets, and return structured metrics plus artifact pointers suitable for Console/Optimize consumption. Endpoint: POST /api/v1/gates/trigger (auth: service-to-service token; scoped to optimize-gating). Request (JSON): gate_tier ("smoke"|"full"), model_id, baseline (baseline_model_version + baseline_runtime_config_ref), proposed (runtime_config_diff, optional proposed_model_version), prompt_sets (ids), sample_count (optional; defaults per tier), run_metadata (route_id, account_id, region, commit_sha, idempotency_key). Validation: limits request payload size, rejects unknown diff keys, enforces that baseline ref is immutable (no "latest"), and checks prompt set ids exist and are enabled for gating. Execution: enqueues a GateRun job to the existing eval runner queue, pins model + runtime settings for the duration of the run, and writes an immutable run record with normalized inputs for audit/debug. Response: 202 Accepted with gate_run_id + status_url + estimated_duration_s; once complete, GET /api/v1/gates/runs/{id} returns status, summary metrics (per prompt set and overall), and artifact pointers (artifact_store_uri + optional signed_url when allowed), including: raw per-example outputs, rubric/score breakdowns, and a compact "comparison.json" (baseline vs proposed deltas). Artifacts: stored under artifact_store_uri prefix gates/{gate_run_id}/..., with retention aligned to existing eval artifact policy; Optimize can deep-link using gate_run_id. Observability: emits gate_trigger_requests_total and gate_run_enqueue_latency_ms (tags: gate_tier, model_id, region, caller=optimize) and attaches trace/span attributes for correlation from Optimize -> eval-harness. Tests: adds contract tests for request validation + schema stability, a fake runner integration test that asserts correct pinning of baseline/proposed configs, and golden JSON snapshots for the run detail response. Notes: This PR does not implement flake scoring/rerun aggregation (tracked in pr-418); response schema includes an optional flake_score field but it is not populated yet. Rollout: endpoint is behind feature flag OPTIMIZE_GATING_TRIGGER_API and requires allowlisted client identity; we can safely deploy without enabling any callers.

source.title: Add token-attribution prober and rollback-suggestion CI adapter
source.author: Ava Chen
source.description:
Motivation: We observed regressions that only manifest at the token level (e.g., a previously rare hallucinated sub-token pattern increasing frequency in certain prompt cohorts). Existing cohort-level SLOs catch change in aggregate metrics but not subtle token-attribution shifts. This PR introduces a lightweight token-attribution prober that runs as part of eval-harness smoke checks and a CI adapter that suggests minimally invasive rollback patches when attribution drift exceeds configured thresholds.

Summary: Adds a token-level attribution probe that integrates model attribution signals (attention-based saliency and logit-difference scoring) into the eval pipeline, produces per-prompt per-token drift heatmaps, and new CI logic that: 1) compares attribution fingerprints to historical anchors, 2) raises soft or hard failures depending on severity, and 3) emits rollback suggestions (prompt edits or anchor-replay patches) for triage. The description below details design, testing, and rollout.

What changed (high level):
- New module eval_harness/probers/token_attribution_prober.py: core probe implementation and pluggable attribution backends.
- New CI adapter eval_harness/ci/adapters/rollback_suggester_adapter.py: consumes probe output and converts detected regressions into actionable suggestions and GitHub-flavored comment payloads for PRs.
- Added a lightweight CLI hook bin/run-token-prober for local reproductions.
- Integrations: hooks registered in eval_harness/orchestrator/hooks.yaml and a new metrics exporter eval_harness/metrics/token_drift_metrics.py.
- Tests: unit and integration tests under tests/probers and tests/ci to validate detection logic and suggestion generation.
- Docs: docs/ci/token-attribution-prober.md with usage, thresholds, and recommended rollout knobs.

How it works (technical):
1) Attribution backends: the prober supports multiple attribution signals. By default we compute token saliency as delta in token logit probability when prefix tokens are ablated (fast approximation implemented) and augment with attention-weighted saliency when available from model traces. Backends are pluggable so teams can add model-specific explainers.
2) Fingerprint generation: for each prompt cohort we compute aggregated token-attribution histograms and normalize them into a fingerprint vector. We store epochal anchors (daily snapshots) and compute a symmetric KL + L2 composite distance to detect shifts.
3) CI gating: adapter compares current fingerprint to the pinned anchor for the target prompt set. Thresholds: soft (suggestion): distance > d_soft (defaults documented); hard (CI fail): distance > d_hard. Soft failures post a triage suggestion comment and attach a mini-report; hard failures block merge.
4) Rollback suggestions: when a soft or hard drift is detected we compute a minimal set of prompt edits or anchor-replay configurations (e.g., revert to prior canonical prompt variant or add an anchor replay entry) and include a suggested patch diff (applies to prompt-set YAMLs) that maintainers can accept with a single click. Suggestions are heuristic and flagged for human review.

Testing & validation:
- Unit tests for attribution math (100% branch coverage for the math kernels).
- Integration test that replays a seeded synthetic drift (see tests/probers/data/synthetic_drift.json) and asserts the adapter flags both soft and hard cases.
- End-to-end run included in CI that executes the prober in Docker with a small open model (weights mocked via deterministic scorer) to keep runtime <90s in smoke runs.

Performance & cost considerations:
- The prober has a fast mode (approximate logit delta with single-token ablations) used in CI smoke runs and a thorough mode (attention saliency + multi-token perturbations) used in nightly anchors. Default CI run uses fast mode to bound cost.

Backward compatibility and rollout plan:
- Default behavior is non-blocking (soft suggestions) for the first two weeks after merge; auto-blocking can be enabled via feature flag in orchestrator config.
- We ship a migration that backfills epochal anchors for the last 14 days; backfill job included at bins/backfill/token_anchor_backfill.py.

Operational signals and dashboards:
- Exposes Prometheus metrics: eval_token_drift_score_total, eval_token_drift_alerts{severity}, eval_token_probe_latency_seconds.
- Added Grafana dashboard JSON (dashboards/token-attribution.json) and an alert rule that triggers SRE paging only for sustained hard failures.

Checklist:
- [x] Implementation code
- [x] Unit & integration tests
- [x] Docs and usage guide
- [x] CI integration and smoke entry
- [x] Rollout plan and backfill job

Notes: This PR intentionally keeps the default thresholds conservative to avoid noisy CI failures during ramp. Teams with sensitive prompt-sets (security/compliance) can opt-in to strict blocking via the orchestrator config.

Related work: This complements the token-saliency smokecheck introduced previously and ties into the cohort-stability framework (ENG-4921) to add a token-granular signal for regression triage.

Manual reproduction: See docs/ci/token-attribution-prober.md for step-by-step; quick reproduction: bin/run-token-prober --cohort tests/probers/data/example_cohort.yaml --mode fast

Merge notes: squash-merged after an extra CI run to ensure the nightly backfill job locks anchors properly.

Ava Chen: Implemented sampling strategy and added determinism seed; updated tests to exercise long prompt path (see commit 4).

Liam O'Connor: CI flake on first run (lint failure); re-ran and green. Approving after test coverage addition.
source.release_notes: Add token-attribution prober and CI rollback suggestion adapter. Introduces token-level drift metrics and non-blocking suggestions by default. Opt-in hard-fail gating available via orchestrator config.
source.notes_for_oncall: If the token-drift alert fires frequently after rollout, flip the adapter to soft-only and run the provided backfill to re-anchor; contact author (Ava) to adjust thresholds.

source.title: Introduce proportional failure heatmap and context coverage CI hook
source.author: Ethan Park
source.release_notes: Add a new CI hook that computes a proportional failure heatmap and context-coverage score per prompt cohort. Soft-fail mode by default; opt-in hard gating available via project config. Adds dashboard panels and backfill script.
source.description: Motivation: some regressions surface only in high-volume contexts or in narrow prompt cohorts; existing eval gates treat each failing example equally which underweights production impact. This PR introduces a proportional failure heatmap and a context-coverage metric that (1) weights failures by frequency and severity, (2) surfaces top-left hotspots in a time-decayed heatmap, and (3) produces a single coverage score used by the CI hook. Design: we cluster prompts into contextual cohorts using the existing prompt fingerprinting + semantic shingle embedding. For each cohort we compute a weighted failure score = sum_{examples in cohort}(failure_weight * recency_decay * frequency_factor). Frequency comes from the sampled traffic histogram (or sample proxy during CI). Severity is derived from configured eval metrics (exact-match, BLEU drop, hallucination flag), mapped to a 1-5 weight. We render a heatmap matrix: rows = cohorts, cols = failure types/buckets, cell = normalized proportional score. The context-coverage score is a normalized aggregate that penalizes under-tested or high-impact cohorts. CI behavior: runs as a post-eval hook. Default is soft-fail (annotate PR and open an advisory alert) and will fail the CI only when coverage < 0.6 AND proportional failure mass > threshold for two consecutive runs. There is an opt-in strict mode in repo eval.yaml to convert soft-fails into hard blocks. Implementation summary: - New hook entry point: eval_harness/hooks/proportional_failure_heatmap.py - Cohort sampler & cache adaptor: eval_harness/samplers/contextual_cohort_sampler.py - Heatmap renderer + export: eval_harness/reporting/coverage_heatmap_v1.py - CLI/backfill helper: tools/heatmap_backfill.py - Dashboard defs: infra/dashboards/coverage-heatmap.json - Unit + integration tests: tests/hooks/test_proportional_heatmap.py + tests/samplers/test_contextual_cohort.py Backwards compatibility: hook uses existing eval outputs; no changes required to model outputs or existing prompt sets. Rollout plan: 1) Merge in soft-fail mode, enable on canary branch for 48 hours (automated job), 2) run backfill for last 30 days using tools/heatmap_backfill.py to populate dashboards, 3) after observing false positive rate < 4% and stable signal for 1 week, flip opt-in projects to strict gating. Checklist: - [x] Unit tests - [x] Integration test (CI job) - [x] Dashboard JSON added - [x] Documentation snippet added to docs/hooks.md - [x] Linear tickets linked (ENG-2345) - [x] Backfill tool included

feat(sampler): introduce contextual cohort sampler with shingle-based clustering
chore(tests): add unit and integration tests for sampler and renderer
perf(cache): add LRU cache wrapper for cohort fingerprints in sampler
docs: document hook usage and rollout guidance in docs/hooks.md
infra: add Grafana dashboard json for coverage heatmap
review_conversation: Maya Gupta: Nice proposal. Can you clarify how frequency_factor is estimated during CI when we don't have live traffic?

Ethan Park: Good question — CI uses a sample-proxy histogram: we synthesize frequency from historical sample rates (last 7 days) stored in the eval cache. If unavailable, a conservative uniform prior is applied and flagged in the report.
Lina Sørensen: The severity mapping table looks arbitrary; please add tests that show sensitivity when weights change.
Ethan Park: Added parameterized tests and a short section in docs explaining how to tune weights per org. Tests cover weight ranges 0.5-3.0 and the coverage score stability.
Rafael Ortega: One nit — avoid expensive tensor ops in the hook path; move heavy compute to background/backfill where possible.
Ethan Park: Moved the full embedding-based clustering to the backfill tool; CI uses cached cluster ids and a cheap hash + shingle fallback. Updated implementation accordingly.
Maya Gupta: Approved after the cache optimization and tests. 👍
ci_checks: integration: initial run failed (flake in test_proportional_heatmap), rerun -> passed
source.merge_outcome: Merged via squash by Maya Gupta. Squash commit message: feat(eval-hook): proportional failure heatmap + coverage CI hook (ENG-2345)
source.notes: This PR intentionally defaults to soft-fail to gather signal before blocking. Projects with high sensitivity to regression should opt into strict gating via eval.yaml. The backfill tool can be rate-limited against production traces — run with caution and on a backfill VM.
source.testing_instructions: Run pytests: pytest tests/hooks/test_proportional_heatmap.py::test_heatmap_aggregation and pytest tests/samplers/test_contextual_cohort.py. To simulate CI behavior locally: python -m eval_harness.hooks.proportional_failure_heatmap --input tests/fixtures/sample_eval_output.json --mode dry-run
source.implementation_notes: Heatmap normalization uses a per-cohort z-score with outlier trimming (winsorize 99th perc). Time decay uses exponential half-life parameter set to 7 days by default. All thresholds are configurable in eval.yaml under hooks.proportional_heatmap.
followup_tasks: ENG-2378: Add adaptive threshold tuning based on historical false positive rate
ENG-2401: Expose cohort exploratory UI in the Console for triage
ENG-2416: Add cross-model failure attribution so heatmap can break down by model version

source.title: Implement prompt fingerprint ledger and replay-safe CI enforcer
source.author: Priya Raman
source.release_notes: Adds a prompt fingerprint ledger and a replay-safe CI enforcer that detects prompt-set composition drift and prevents spurious eval regressions; includes opt-in backfill tooling and operational metrics.
source.description:
Motivation: Flaky eval failures at the prompt-set level continue to surface from subtle dataset composition drift (sampling, paraphrase swaps, and tokenization edge cases) rather than model regressions. This PR implements a compact on-disk ledger and a CI-side enforcer that (1) records stable fingerprints for prompt-sets+seed+model-config, (2) compares new runs against the ledger to flag composition-driven variance, and (3) provides a replay-safe enforcement mode that permits human review and controlled backfills before a hard CI failure.

Design summary: The fingerprint is a deterministic, salted hash that combines prompt identifiers, normalized prompt text, canonical tokenization signature, run-seed, and a lightweight output sketch (sampled token-level checksums plus aggregated metrics). A new ledger store (local by default; support for S3/GCS) holds one canonical entry per evaluated prompt-set+baseline ID. The CI enforcer (eval_ci_enforcer.py) is a thin adapter that runs after eval jobs complete: it computes fingerprints for the run, diffs against the ledger, and decides pass/warn/fail according to policy. Policies are configurable via the existing eval configuration (eval_config.yaml) with three modes: off, warn-only, and replay-safe-enforce. source.title: Introduce anchor-driven failure replay with progressive quarantine throttler
source.author: Ethan Park
source.ci_status: pass (pipeline retried after two flaky integration failures)
source.release_notes: Introduces an anchor-based failure replay component and a progressive quarantine throttler for eval CI: reduces noisy rollbacks by replaying anchored failures deterministically and throttling problematic prompt cohorts; adds a new CI job 'eval-anchor-replay' and observability tags for anchored replay runs.
source.description: Motivation: Spikes of noisy eval failures (often transient or infra-related) are triggering rollbacks and incorrect regression signals. This PR introduces an anchor-driven failure replay system that replays a small, provenance-anchored sample of failing requests against the candidate and baseline in isolation, plus a progressive quarantine throttler that limits the blast radius of cohorts that produce repeated non-deterministic failures. Key goals: reduce noisy CI failures, improve root-cause signal for real regressions, and keep reruns bounded and auditable. What this changes: 1) eval_harness/orchestrator/anchor_replay.py: new replay engine that captures request/response provenance and replays deterministically with KV-cache seeding; 2) ci/hooks/anchor_replay_hook.py: CI hook that marks failing eval jobs for anchored replay and triggers the replay job; 3) ci/configs/progressive_quarantine.yaml: tunable policy for tiered quarantine (soft hold -> partial throttle -> full quarantine) with exponential backoff and decay; 4) metrics: adds eval.anchor_replay.* tags, replay_outcome, quarantine_state and quarantine_duration to perf telemetry; 5) tests: integration and unit tests covering deterministic replay paths and quarantine transitions under simulated flakiness; 6) docs/ci/anchor-replay.md: operational runbook and troubleshooting. Backwards compatibility: the hook is opt-in by default for projects; existing eval pipelines are unchanged until the new ci job is enabled. Checklist: - [x] Design doc linked (ENG-1987) - [x] Unit + integration tests added - [x] Metrics and traces emitted - [x] Opt-in config and sane defaults - [x] Docs and runbook included How it works (brief): when an eval job fails, the hook attaches a compact anchor snapshot (seed prompt IDs + minimal context + tokenization fingerprints). The anchor-replay job takes those anchors, rehydrates deterministic inputs (fixing RNG seeds, KV cache priming) and runs the candidate and baseline in an isolated environment with strict timeouts and dual-run tracing. If a replay confirms a regression we escalate; if a replay is unstable (non-deterministic) we transition the cohort through the quarantine throttler which progressively reduces traffic routed to the cohort for live shadowing and increases sampling frequency for targeted repro attempts. Observability and safety: replay runs are tagged and stored, and the runbook documents triage steps to avoid accidental rollouts based solely on single-run failures. Performance considerations: replay is intentionally small (anchors limited by size and capped to N per job) to keep cost low; quarantine throttler uses exponential backoff to avoid constant reruns. Related work: builds on ideas in ENG-2042 (anchored probes) and the regression-blame tooling (prior PR).

d7c1b05: introduce CI hook and eval job integration
c3e9bf1: add progressive_quarantine policy and config schema
f4126a0: tests for replay determinism and quarantine transitions
e88c02d: docs: anchor-replay runbook and example configs
source.diff_summary: Added ~1.2k LOC primarily implementing anchor replay and quarantine policy. New CI job + config. Tests + docs included. Small refactor to orchestrator to surface anchor metadata.
review_comments: Leah Chen: Can the anchor snapshot be further size-bounded? I saw a case where context blowup could make anchors large. Suggest enforcing token limit and dropping large attachments.
Ethan Park: Good point — implemented a token cap and gzip-on-write for anchor payloads in commit d7c1b05. Updated docs to note the cap.
Marco Ruiz: Integration tests are flaky in CI (timeout on replay run). Please add a shorter timeout variant for CI to avoid long-stalled jobs.
Ethan Park: Added a CI-only timeout override and a debug flag to collect replay traces on timeout in f4126a0.
Priya Nair: How does quarantine interact with canary rollouts? We should avoid silently throttling a canary route.
Ethan Park: Quarantine is not applied to explicit canary routes by default; added an opt-in flag in the config and clarified in the runbook.
Marco Ruiz: Approve after CI re-run — last build had two flaky integration failures but core unit tests look good.
Leah Chen: Approving. Observability hooks look sufficient to triage future incidents.
source.merge: squash and merge by Marco Ruiz. Commit message consolidated; merged to main with merge commit referencing ENG-1987 and ENG-2042.
source.notes_for_mergers: This change is opt-in. When enabling, monitor eval.anchor_replay.replay_outcome and quarantine_state metrics for three days. Engineering oncall: @marco_ruiz and @leah_chen.
source.operational_playbook: docs/ci/anchor-replay.md contains steps: reproduce locally with examples/anchor-sample.json, triggering replay job, triage decision tree for confirmed regression vs flake, and rollback guidance.
