source.title: Introduce contextualized minibatch regression watchdog and suppression adapter
source.author: Asha Menon
source.description:
Motivation: Flaky per-prompt regressions are noisy in CI when single minibatch failures trigger rollbacks or high-severity alerts. This PR adds a contextualized minibatch regression watchdog that computes short-horizon, context-aware regression signals and a suppression adapter that dampens alerts from transient minibatch noise. The goal is to reduce false positives in CI gating while preserving sensitivity to sustained quality regressions.

What this PR introduces: 1) src/watchdogs/contextual_minibatch_watchdog.py - new watchdog which calculates per-prompt-context rolling statistics (short window mean, variance, exponential smoothing) and emits a scored regression signal. 2) src/adapters/suppression_adapter.py - a pluggable adapter used in CI and alerting pipelines to apply hysteresis and suppression rules: confirmation windows, adaptive thresholds based on context prevalence, and a backoff multiplier for high-variance prompts. 3) src/metrics/contextual_variance_aggregator.py - a lightweight aggregator that computes cross-seed/context variance and integrates with existing EvalMetric interface. 4) ci/hooks/watchdog-ci.yml - CI hook that runs a focused smoke eval using minibatch shards and gates on sustained signal (configured as 3 consecutive windows). 5) tests/{unit,integration}/watchdog_* - unit and integration tests covering edge cases (all-zero tokens, variable batch sizes, missing contexts), plus a flakiness simulator for synthetic load.

Design notes: - The watchdog uses context keys derived from prompt metadata (domain, prompt_type, client_tier) so statistics are computed in semantically meaningful buckets. Bucketing is configurable at runtime. - Suppression adapter policy: require confirmation over N windows or N distinct shards before escalating. N defaults to 3 but is policy-driven per repo/route. - CI hook semantics: the CI job will fail only if the suppression adapter permits escalation. The default CI configuration is conservative (designed to reduce developer churn) but project leads can opt-in to stricter thresholds.

Compatibility and rollout: - Backwards compatible: new modules expose adapter hooks but do not change existing evaluation behavior unless enabled via config. - Rolling deployment plan: (a) land code behind feature flag, (b) enable on small set of noisy prompt-sets for 2 weeks, (c) expand to all smoke CI jobs if no missed regressions detected.

Checklist (manual): - [x] Unit tests for watchdog logic and aggregator - [x] Integration test that simulates transient flakiness and verifies suppression - [x] CI job definition and README for ops - [x] Documentation note in docs/eval/watchdogs.md (referenced in PR body)

Notes: This PR focuses on detection and suppression for minibatch-level noise patterns. It intentionally avoids hard-coded model-level thresholds and instead exposes configuration knobs for per-team policy tuning.

source.title: Add ensemble-score-aggregator and CI regression hook for composite eval metrics
source.author: Maya Patel
source.description: Motivation: teams running multi-metric evals need a single, auditable signal for CI gating that reflects weighted regressions across prompt sets and metric families. This PR introduces an "ensemble score" aggregator that computes a weighted composite from a configurable set of metrics (e.g. BLEU, ROUGE-L, exact-match, embedding-sim) and a CI hook that fails builds when the ensemble score or any high-priority submetric regresses past configured thresholds. What changed: added aggregator implementation, config schema, CI integration, unit + integration tests, docs and migration notes. Checklist: [x] implementation, [x] schema & defaults, [x] unit tests, [x] integration test against sample prompt-sets, [x] CI job wired to GitHub Actions, [x] docs and example config in docs/evals. Related: Linear ENG-2141, ENG-2210; references: https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-2141/ensemble-eval-signal and design doc link (internal).

chore(config): extend eval-config schema with ensemble_score definition and thresholds
test(ci): add CI hook and integration test harness using sample prompt bundles
docs: document ensemble score behavior and migration guide
fix(tests): stabilize timing in integration test by seeding RNG and mocking latency
review_comments: Diego: Please explain how weights are chosen and persisted across runs; consider deterministic tie-breaks in aggregator.
Maya: Added schema docs and a migration guide; default weights are conservative and included in examples. Persisted via eval-run metadata. Added deterministic stable-sort for ties.
Ava: CI job looks good but flakiness on integration test observed locally; recommend mocking network calls.
Maya: Mocked external calls and seeded RNGs; reran pipeline. All green.
Liam: Approve after tests stabilized and docs added.
source.release_notes: Introduces ensemble scoring for multi-metric evaluations and a CI hook that gates on composite/regression thresholds. Provides config schema and docs; may require downstream eval configs to opt in.
source.notes_for_ops: Ops should add ensemble thresholds to protected repos if they want CI gating; default behavior is opt-in. CI job eval-regression.yml added to standard workflow; ensure runners have python3.11 and eval-harness dependencies installed.
source.risk_assessment: Low-medium: code paths for existing single-metric evals unchanged. New aggregator has unit coverage and integration tests. Potential for false positives if weights misconfigured; mitigated by opt-in default and clear docs.

source.title: introduce-historical-variance-detector-and-variance-gate-hook
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.release_notes: Adds a historical variance detector to the eval harness and a new CI gating hook that fails PRs when prompt-set metric variance exceeds configurable thresholds; includes new tests and a small CLI flag for local evaluations.
source.description:
Motivation: We've seen intermittent metric oscillations across repeated eval runs (same prompt set, different seeds / sampling windows) that cause nondeterministic gate failures and noisy postdeploy regressions. This PR introduces a Historical Variance Detector (HVD) to detect when a prompt-set or cohort exhibits unstable score distributions over a sliding window of previous eval runs and optionally block merges.

What I changed (high level):
- Add a new module eval_harness/variance_detector.py that computes per-prompt and cohort-level variance, runs a two-sample KS test against the historical distribution, and produces a compact variance report.
- Add an opt-in GH Action workflow .github/workflows/variance-gate.yml that invokes the harness check with the new --variance-check flag on PRs targeting main and release/*.
- Extend the CLI: `harness check --suite <suite> --variance-check --lookback 8 --alpha 0.01 --delta-threshold 0.12` (defaults chosen to be conservative). The CLI returns non-zero if any cohort/prompt exceeds thresholds.
- Integrate an experimental `rollback_scorer` that surfaces the top-N prompts/cohorts contributing to variance; this hooks into the existing rollout recommendations UI (backend only) so SRE/Platform can inspect before rolling forward.
- Tests: add unit tests for distribution comparisons and an integration test that runs against a synthetic history store. Also add a small compatibility shim for older historical-run artifacts.

Commits (summary):
- c1f8b2e: initial variance-detector implementation + docs
- 7a2d9f4: add KS test and sliding-window logic; expose lookback/alpha flags
- e3b4a01: GH Action workflow for variance gate + small CI adapter
- 9f0a5c7: integrate rollback_scorer and add API surface for console
- f1d3b6a: tests and flaky-seed fix (see review notes)

Files changed (high level):
- eval_harness/variance_detector.py (+320 additions)
- eval_harness/cli.py (+18 additions) — new flags and wiring
- integration_tests/test_variance_gate.py (+42 additions)
- .github/workflows/variance-gate.yml (+45 additions)
- docs/variance.md (+30 additions)
- eval_harness/history/shim_compat.py (+20 additions)
- eval_harness/rollback_scorer.py (+35 additions)

Design notes and rationale: HVD is deliberately conservative: it only gates when both the normalized variance and a KS-test p-value indicate distributional shift beyond the delta threshold. This reduces false positives from isolated outliers. The sliding window defaults to 8 most recent eval runs (empirically chosen from internal history), but is configurable per-suite. We log compact histograms and top-contributors to make triage actionable.

Review conversation (abridged, chronological):
- Ethan Cole: "Can the detector be made resilient to differing sample sizes? If historical runs have different numbers of completions we should normalize or bootstrap." PRIYA: "Implemented sample-size-aware bootstrapped CIs in the latest commit; added integration test to cover uneven sizes." (commit 7a2d9f4)
- Maya Chen: "Please parameterize lookback and alpha at the suite level in addition to CLI flags; also document expected run cadence." PRIYA: "Added suite-level defaults in suite_config and updated docs/variance.md with recommended cadences." (commit e3b4a01)
- Luca Bianchi: "Flaky integration test: failing intermittently on CI due to RNG. Can we seed or use deterministic sampling?" PRIYA: "Fixed by seeding the synthetic generator and switching from random.gauss to numpy's Generator with PCG64 for deterministic draws in tests." (commit f1d3b6a)
- Maya Chen: "Approving — please squash on merge; would like a follow-up to wire alerts into Slack ops channel." (approval)
- Ethan Cole: "LGTM after one nit about log verbosity; not blocking." (approval)

CI & gating behavior observed on this PR:
- First run: variance-gate workflow failed due to the flaky integration test (intermittent rng). See job variance-integration (failed).
- Fix applied (seed deterministic RNG) and tests updated; second run: all checks passed (unit, integration, lint).
- ci_status: pass

Edge cases & limitations called out in review: historical store retention matters — if history is sparse the detector will gracefully degrade to a per-run warning mode rather than hard-failure. The KS test assumes continuous distributions; for very small sample sizes the detector falls back to a median-delta check (documented in docs/variance.md).

Compatibility & rollout plan: The variance gate is opt-in by default. We enabled it for the eval-harness repo in team CI to evaluate noise levels for 2 weeks. After that, teams can opt their suites into enforce=true to block merges automatically. The rollback_scorer is experimental and surfaced only as a backend API for now; Console integration will follow as a separate PR (ENG-4302).

Merge: Squash merge after approvals. Merged at 2025-11-05. Follow-ups: ENG-4302 (console UI), ENG-4311 (Slack alerts), and a cross-repo checklist to enable enforcement for prioritized suites.

Notes for QA/Oncall: If adopters see increased gate failures, check eval-harness history retention and per-suite lookback config. For urgent rollbacks, use the rollback_scorer API to identify candidate prompts to revert or to create targeted prompt filters.
notes: This PR is intentionally conservative about gating and includes an opt-in path.
Follow-on work tracked in ENG-4302 and ENG-4311.

source.title: Tag eval runs with model version + model-metadata schema hash
source.author: Jonas Weber
source.description: Context: As part of the Model Metadata Schema v1 + automated changelog work, we need to be able to trace a customer-visible release note entry back to concrete eval evidence (and vice versa). Today the eval harness records the model identifier and some run params, but does not persist (a) the promoted model version string used for serving (e.g., catalog version/variant) and (b) the model-metadata schema hash that the changelog generator references. That makes it hard to answer support questions like “which exact model version did this eval run validate?” and blocks automated linking in release notes. What this PR does: 1) Adds required fields to the eval run manifest/results payload: model_version (string) and model_metadata_schema_hash (sha256 hex). 2) Populates these fields from the run config when available, and falls back to resolving via the model registry client (best-effort) when the caller provides model_id but not a version. 3) Propagates the tags to: run.json, summary.json, and the top-level artifact index so downstream tooling (changelog generator / Console) can join on (model_version, schema_hash) without parsing logs. 4) Adds CLI flags + env var support: --model-version, --model-metadata-schema-hash, with REDWOOD_MODEL_VERSION / REDWOOD_MODEL_METADATA_SCHEMA_HASH overrides (CI-friendly). 5) Adds validation: schema hash must be 64-char hex; model_version must be non-empty and match our semver-ish format (allows catalog suffixes like "+q4_k_m"). Compatibility: Existing stored runs remain readable; the reader treats missing fields as "unknown" and emits a warning (no hard failure). New runs will include the fields. Testing: unit tests for validation + serialization; integration test that runs a tiny harness workflow and asserts artifacts contain tags; golden-file update for run manifest. Rollout notes: we should update the CI templates in redwood-model-registry and the internal eval job definitions to pass the schema hash (follow-up in ENG-9827 checklist).
commits: eval/manifest: add model_version + model_metadata_schema_hash fields to RunManifest + serialization
cli: plumb through --model-version/--model-metadata-schema-hash + env var overrides
registry-client: best-effort resolve version when only model_id is provided (no network call when version already supplied)
artifacts: include tags in artifact index + summary.json
tests: add validation coverage + update golden fixtures
files_changed: src/eval_harness/cli/run.py (new flags, env var support, wiring to config)
conversation: Quentin Harris (review): Can we align the schema hash definition with model-metadata-schema-v1.json? Specifically: is this the JSON Schema file hash, the registry schema version, or a hash of a normalized schema representation? We need this to match what changelog generator stores.

Jonas Weber (author): Good call. In this PR it is the sha256 of the canonical JSON Schema file bytes as published by the model registry repo. I added a comment + doc text to make it explicit, and renamed internal variable from schema_version_hash -> model_metadata_schema_hash for clarity.

Sana Farid (review): Please avoid hard validation on model_version format that could block experimental variants. Also: can we include both “resolved” and “requested” version? (we’ve had jobs pass “latest”).

Jonas Weber (author): I loosened validation to only require non-empty string and added a warning-only regex check (does not fail the run). For “latest”: I added fields model_version_requested (optional) and model_version_resolved (the persisted model_version). To keep the external interface simple, we still expose model_version as the resolved value; docs updated. (Note: both are flat strings in the JSON artifacts.)
Logan Wright (review): Network calls during eval run start worry me (rate limits + flakiness). Can we make registry resolution opt-in and default off in local runs?

Jonas Weber (author): Done. Resolution requires explicit --resolve-model-version or REDWOOD_RESOLVE_MODEL_VERSION=1; otherwise we only use provided values. Integration tests run with the resolver off.
Michael Nguyen (review): Add a test for the redaction boundary: schema hash is fine, but make sure we never log internal registry URLs/tokens into artifacts.
Jonas Weber (author): Added test asserting artifacts don’t contain REGISTRY_BASE_URL and ensured token is never included in RunManifest (only used in client auth header).
Sana Farid (approve): Approved after latest changes. Please file a follow-up to update the release-notes generator join logic once artifacts are in prod.
Jonas Weber (author): Follow-up noted in ENG-9827 checklist; will coordinate with Michael/Quentin.
source.release_notes: Eval harness now persists model_version + model_metadata_schema_hash in eval artifacts to enable traceability from model release notes to supporting eval evidence.

source.title: Introduce contextual ballot evaluator and safety-snapshot regression gate
source.author: Maya Chen
source.description:
Motivation: CI-driven functional regressions occasionally pass simple metric thresholds but still cause low-probability failure modes (safety, hallucination) to regress. This PR adds two complementary CI primitives: a Contextual Ballot Evaluator (CBE) that runs configurable re-samples of model responses and decides pass/fail by consensus ballots weighted by semantic similarity and prompt cohort importance; and a Safety Snapshot Gate that captures classifier score distributions at merge time and raises soft-fail alerts when tail mass shifts beyond tolerance.

What this change does: Adds a ballot aggregation engine with pluggable weighting (semantic similarity, precedent weight, and token-length normalization), a CI hook that executes N reruns in parallel with staggered seeds and computes vote distributions, and a safety snapshot artifact that is stored alongside CI artifacts for historical comparison. The gate emits structured signals consumed by the eval orchestrator to trigger soft quarantine or canary-focused rollouts.

Implementation notes: The ballot evaluator is implemented in eval_harness/core/ballot_evaluator.py with deterministic pseudo-random seeding and a replayable audit log. The CI hook lives in eval_harness/hooks/ci_ballot_hook.py and integrates with existing smoke runner. Safety snapshots are compact JSON braindumps capturing classifier score quantiles and cohort-level anchors and are emitted by eval_harness/hooks/safety_snapshot.py. Unit and integration tests added under tests/ to validate aggregation logic and snapshot diffing.

Checklist:
- [x] Ballot evaluator core logic and weighting strategies implemented
- [x] CI hook and pipeline integration added (ci/pipeline.yaml changes)
- [x] Safety snapshot emitter and historical comparator implemented
- [x] Tests added: unit, integration, snapshot regression simulation
- [x] Docs: docs/eval-playbooks/ballot_and_safety.md with operational guidance

Related work: builds on prior CI gating work (ENG-7203) and the contextual anchor patterns in ENG-7321. Adds an alternative to strict-threshold gates by providing probabilistic consensus and explicit snapshot-based drift detection.

Commit summary: Implement ballot aggregator and CI hook; Add safety snapshot writer and comparator; Add tests and mock fixtures; Update CI pipeline to run ballot hook on slow smoke; Add playbook docs with example linear job and runbook.

Merge decision: After two rounds of review and a CI rerun for flaky test coverage, reviewers approved and the PR was squashed and merged.

9a1f5b7 Add CI ballot hook and pipeline integration

title: Introduce contextual ballot evaluator and safety-snapshot regression gate
preview:
Motivation: CI-driven functional regressions occasionally pass simple metric thresholds but still cause low-probability failure modes (safety, hallucination) to regress. This PR adds two complementary CI primitives: a Contextual Ballot Evaluator (CBE) that runs configurable re-samples of model responses and decides pass/fail by consensus ballots weighted by semantic similarity and prompt cohort importance; and a Safety Snapshot Gate that captures classifier score distributions at merge time and raises soft-fail alerts when tail mass shifts beyond tolerance.

What this change does: Adds a ballot aggregation engine with pluggable weighting (semantic similarity, precedent weight, and token-length normalization), a CI hook that executes N reruns in parallel with staggered seeds and computes vote distributions, and a safety snapshot artifact that is stored alongside CI artifacts for historical comparison. The gate emits structured signals consumed by the eval orchestrator to trigger soft quarantine or canary-focused rollouts.

Implementation notes: The ballot evaluator is implemented in eval_harness/core/ballot_evaluator.py with deterministic pseudo-random seeding and a replayable audit log. The CI hook lives in eval_harness/hooks/ci_ballot_hook.py and integrates with existing smoke runner. Safety snapshots are compact JSON braindumps capturing classifier score quantiles and cohort-level anchors and are emitted by eval_harness/hooks/safety_snapshot.py. Unit and integration tests added under tests/ to validate aggregation logic and snapshot diffing.

Checklist:
- [x] Ballot evaluator core logic and weighting strategies implemented
- [x] CI hook and pipeline integration added (ci/pipeline.yaml changes)
- [x] Safety snapshot emitter and historical comparator implemented
- [x] Tests added: unit, integration, snapshot regression simulation
- [x] Docs: docs/eval-playbooks/ballot_and_safety.md with operational guidance

Related work: builds on prior CI gating work (ENG-7203) and the contextual anchor patterns in ENG-7321. Adds an alternative to strict-threshold gates by providing probabilistic consensus and explicit snapshot-based drift detection.

Commit summary: Implement ballot aggregator and CI hook; Add safety snapshot writer and comparator; Add tests and mock fixtures; Update CI pipeline to run ballot hook on slow smoke; Add playbook docs with example linear job and runbook.

Merge decision: After two rounds of review and a CI rerun for flaky test coverage, reviewers approved and the PR was squashed and merged.

source.title: Introduce staggered probing schedule and failure-decay gate
source.author: Priya Menon
source.body:
Summary: Adds a time-staggered probing scheduler and a failure-decay gate to the eval CI pipeline. The staggered prober spreads probe execution across configurable windows to avoid thundering on sensitive model endpoints and to produce smoother rolling signals. The failure-decay gate introduces an exponentially decaying penalty for isolated failures so that transient noise does not immediately block rollouts.
Motivation: We observed several incidents where atomic spikes in flakiness (network blips, transient infra GC, tokenization flakiness) caused immediate hard failures in the regression gate, triggering noisy rollbacks. This change reduces false-positive blocks while preserving sensitivity to sustained regressions.
Design: - StaggeredProber: new scheduler that accepts a probe set and a window configuration (slot_count, jitter_seed, priority_buckets). It assigns probes to slots deterministically by shard key (prompt set id) with optional random jitter to smooth correlations. - FailureDecayGate: tracks per-probe failure scores and computes a smoothed failure rate using exponential decay (alpha-configurable). Gate fails only when the decayed failure rate exceeds threshold for N consecutive evaluation windows. - Integration: The prober plugs into the existing eval orchestrator as an alternate fanout mode; the gate is evaluated as part of the cluster of CI hooks after aggregated metrics step.
What changed (high level): - Added evaluator/scheduler/staggered_prober.py (core scheduling logic). - Added hooks/gates/failure_decay_gate.py (gate implementation and configuration). - Augmented orchestration: eval_orchestrator now supports --probe-mode staggered and plumbs a per-run window id. - New config: ci_defaults.yml has staggered defaults and decay params. - Tests: unit tests for scheduling determinism, integration test that simulates transient failures and verifies gate behaviour.
Rollout/flags: Default is disabled. Feature flag eval.staggered_prober enabled for canary fleets. FailureDecayGate enabled behind eval.failure_decay boolean; we will turn on for shadow runs for 72 hours before gating mainline merges.
Testing performed: - Local unit tests + mocked clock deterministic tests (added). - End-to-end integration in staging with a synthetic noisy backend reproduced the old false-positive pattern; the new gate retained sensitivity to sustained regressions while ignoring isolated spikes. - Load test: staggered mode reduced peak concurrent probe workers by ~68% under our synthetic sharded workload.
Observability and telemetry: - New metrics: eval.staggered.slots_utilization, eval.staggered.skew_conflict_rate, eval.failure_decay.decayed_rate, eval.failure_decay.window_alarms. - Added dashboard panels and SLO alert tweaks in observability-pack to avoid duplicate alerts during initial rollout.
Backwards compatibility: Existing probe modes (batch, immediate) unchanged. Gate is additive and can be toggled per-project.
Linear/Jira: Implements ENG-3421 (reduce noisy regression blocks) and ENG-3570 (probe scheduling improvements); related to SUP-8891 (customer-reported flakiness).
Risks and mitigations: - Misconfiguration could delay detection of true regressions: mitigated by conservative default thresholds and gradual rollout. - Scheduler determinism across workers: covered by deterministic sharding tests and cross-checked with end-to-end runs.
Checklist: - [x] Unit tests added - [x] Integration test added - [x] Docs updated (ci/README section) - [x] Feature flags wired - [x] Telemetry emitted
Release notes: "Introduce staggered probing schedule to reduce probe thundering and a failure-decay gating mechanism to reduce false-positive CI blocks. Disabled by default; enable via feature flags for canaries."

b2c3d7e: add failure-decay gate and integrate into orchestrator

source.title: Add anchorwise fidelity audit and CI explainability hook
source.author: Maya Chen
source.description:
Motivation: Teams running long-lived prompt sets have asked for more granular, explainable regression signals tied to 'anchors' (representative examples) so that CI failures are actionable without manual triage. This PR introduces an anchorwise fidelity audit that computes per-anchor fidelity and saliency deltas, surfaces interpretable failure reasons (e.g., token shift, hallucinated span, idempotency drop), and wires a CI hook that annotates PRs with a compact explainability report.

What this changes: adds a lightweight audit runner (hooks/anchorwise_audit.py) that: (1) replays the prompt-set against a reference baseline and the candidate change, (2) computes per-anchor metrics (fidelity_score, token_saliency_delta, semantic_overlap_pct), (3) runs a stacked heuristic classifier to attribute probable failure cause, and (4) emits a normalized JSON+CSV summary consumed by the CI hook. The CI hook (ci/hooks/anchor_explainability_gate.yml) runs in the eval pipeline and will soft-fail on degradations exceeding configured thresholds and post an explainability comment to the PR with the top 5 anchors requiring attention.

Checklist (implementation & rollout):
- [x] Core audit runner implementation with unit tests
- [x] CI hook definition and comment poster integration
- [x] Documentation: docs/anchorwise_fidelity.md with metric definitions and remediation guidance
- [x] Backwards-compatible config: default thresholds set to conservative values and overridable via .evalrc in repo
- [x] Smoke run against recent canary commits to validate signal and false-positive rate
- [x] Telemetry hooks for false-positive collection and opt-out flag

Testing & validation: ran unit tests locally and in CI; performed a 48-hour shadow run on the redwood/main prompt set (shadow run id: shadow-20250318-02) to confirm the anchor ranking aligns with human triage in >85% of sampled cases. Thresholds were tuned to target ~5% soft-fail rate on historical diffs.

Rollout plan: merge to main with feature flagged disabled by default. Flip feature flag for eval-maintainers and run as hard gate for a single team for two weeks before organization-wide opt-in.

Risks and mitigations: heuristics can be noisy for very small anchors (<= 3 tokens); we filter those out and include a warning in the audit output. The CI hook is soft-fail by default to avoid blocking regressions in flight.
commits: 6a4f3b2: initial anchorwise audit runner skeleton and interfaces
b9d1c7e: compute fidelity_score, token_saliency_delta, semantic_overlap modules
d2f8a11: CI hook for posting explainability report and threshold config
f3c7b90: add unit tests and fixtures for 12 anchor archetypes
a1b2c3d: docs for metrics, remediation guidance, and feature flagging

Maya Chen: Added a conservative status line in the docs and the CI hook limits runs to sampled anchors by default (configurable).
Priya Nair: The attribution heuristics look useful, but we should expose a per-anchor confidence score so triage queues can sort.
Maya Chen: Added confidence field to CSV/JSON output and updated tests.
Luca Romano: CI flake on lint in the first run — re-run cleared it after formatting fix. Approved after docs update.

source.title: Tag eval outputs with experiment variant identifiers + publish compact score stream for Console
source.author: Jonas Weber
source.description:
Motivation: Route-level A/B testing in Console needs a quality signal per {route, experiment_id, variant_id}. Today, eval-harness produces per-run artifacts (full JSON + HTML reports) but those are not consistently attributable to a routing experiment variant, and they’re too heavy for Console ingestion. This PR (1) propagates experiment attribution fields into eval execution and output artifacts, and (2) publishes a compact score stream suitable for near-real-time aggregation and dashboarding.

What’s included: (A) Variant tagging: add optional experiment context to eval runs, including experiment_id, variant_id, route_id, and a stable stickiness_key_hash (when provided), and persist these tags into (i) run metadata.json, (ii) per-prompt result rows, and (iii) summary.json. (B) Compact score stream: emit one record per {run_id, prompt_set_id, scorer_name} with normalized score, raw score, and minimal dimensions. This is designed to be ingested by Telemetry/Analytics and joined with experiment guardrails results without pulling full eval artifacts.

Schema (score stream v1): Each record is JSONL with fields: event_type=eval_score_v1, emitted_at, run_id, eval_suite_id, prompt_set_id, scorer_name, score_value, score_value_normalized, normalization_version, sample_n, model_id, model_revision, region (if applicable), route_id (optional), experiment_id (optional), variant_id (optional), attribution_source (router|manual|unknown), and trace_id (optional). Notes: (1) experiment_id/variant_id are omitted (not null) when not available to reduce payload size and simplify downstream cardinality controls; (2) scorer_name is restricted to an allowlist configured in eval-harness to prevent uncontrolled dimensionality.

Integration points: (1) CLI: new flags --experiment-id, --variant-id, --route-id, --attribution-source. (2) Runner API: EvalRunContext gains optional ExperimentAttribution. (3) Publishers: new ScoreStreamPublisher (default off) that can write to stdout (dev), file (integration tests), or the internal bus topic eval-score-stream-v1 (prod). The publisher is behind a config gate (EVAL_SCORE_STREAM_ENABLED) and can be enabled per environment.

Safety/rollout: Backward compatible. Existing artifact formats remain valid; we only add new keys. The score stream publisher is disabled by default. Added guardrails to avoid emitting records when scorer output is missing/NaN. Added sampling controls for very large prompt sets: emit summary scores always; per-prompt scores remain only in artifacts, not in the stream.

Testing: Added unit tests for attribution propagation, JSON schema validation tests for stream records, and an end-to-end fixture that runs a tiny suite and asserts that emitted JSONL contains correct experiment_id/variant_id. Also added a compatibility test to ensure older consumers parsing metadata.json don’t break.

Follow-ups (not in this PR): (1) finalize Console-facing ‘quality’ metric mapping and thresholds (Applied ML + Product); (2) telemetry aggregation jobs (ENG-9027) to roll up score stream by {experiment_id, variant_id}; (3) documentation updates for customers/internal operators on how evals attach to experiments.

Risk notes: Cardinality is controlled by keeping the stream to a small dimension set; do not add raw prompt text or user identifiers. stickiness_key_hash is salted and truncated to prevent correlation across customers while allowing debugging for a single run.
commits: eval: add ExperimentAttribution to run context and artifact writers
cli: flags for experiment_id/variant_id/route_id + attribution_source
publisher: implement ScoreStreamPublisher + JSONL serializer (v1)
tests: schema validation + e2e fixture for score stream output
docs: internal README for score stream contract and enablement

Jonas Weber (author): Yes—added an allowlist in config (scorers.allowlisted_for_stream). Non-allowlisted scorers still appear in full artifacts but are skipped for the stream with a warning counter.
Logan Wright: Please avoid emitting nulls for optional dimensions; our ingestion treats null vs missing differently and it can double series count.
Jonas Weber (author): Updated serializer to omit experiment_id/variant_id/route_id when not present. Added a test asserting key omission.
Caleb Johnson: We should include a schema_version field for forward compatibility (even if it’s v1).

Selene Huang: For quality regression alerts we’ll need normalized score + normalization_version. Also need sample_n to interpret noise.
Jonas Weber (author): Both included. Added doc comment on normalization_version semantics and how to bump it.
Olivia Grant: Small nit: route_id should be the normalized route template, not raw path. Where does this come from?

Jonas Weber (author): The harness takes route_id from the caller (router integration). Added a note in README: route_id must be normalized upstream per platform conventions; harness will not accept raw paths.
source.requested_changes: Requested changes addressed: (1) scorer allowlist to limit dimensionality; (2) omit optional keys rather than emitting null; (3) add schema_version; (4) add README with producer contract + enablement; (5) add E2E test fixture.

source.rollout_plan: 1) Merge behind config gate (this PR). 2) Enable in staging for route experiments beta suites only (Applied ML). 3) Verify ingestion + aggregation with Telemetry (watch for cardinality + drop rate). 4) Enable in prod for selected suites; expand once Console results view consumes score stream.

source.merge_outcome: Merged to main via squash by Olivia Grant after approvals from Applied ML + Telemetry reviewers.

title: Tag eval outputs with experiment variant identifiers + publish compact score stream for Console
preview:
Motivation: Route-level A/B testing in Console needs a quality signal per {route, experiment_id, variant_id}. Today, eval-harness produces per-run artifacts (full JSON + HTML reports) but those are not consistently attributable to a routing experiment variant, and they’re too heavy for Console ingestion. This PR (1) propagates experiment attribution fields into eval execution and output artifacts, and (2) publishes a compact score stream suitable for near-real-time aggregation and dashboarding.

What’s included: (A) Variant tagging: add optional experiment context to eval runs, including experiment_id, variant_id, route_id, and a stable stickiness_key_hash (when provided), and persist these tags into (i) run metadata.json, (ii) per-prompt result rows, and (iii) summary.json. (B) Compact score stream: emit one record per {run_id, prompt_set_id, scorer_name} with normalized score, raw score, and minimal dimensions. This is designed to be ingested by Telemetry/Analytics and joined with experiment guardrails results without pulling full eval artifacts.

Schema (score stream v1): Each record is JSONL with fields: event_type=eval_score_v1, emitted_at, run_id, eval_suite_id, prompt_set_id, scorer_name, score_value, score_value_normalized, normalization_version, sample_n, model_id, model_revision, region (if applicable), route_id (optional), experiment_id (optional), variant_id (optional), attribution_source (router|manual|unknown), and trace_id (optional). Notes: (1) experiment_id/variant_id are omitted (not null) when not available to reduce payload size and simplify downstream cardinality controls; (2) scorer_name is restricted to an allowlist configured in eval-harness to prevent uncontrolled dimensionality.

Integration points: (1) CLI: new flags --experiment-id, --variant-id, --route-id, --attribution-source. (2) Runner API: EvalRunContext gains optional ExperimentAttribution. (3) Publishers: new ScoreStreamPublisher (default off) that can write to stdout (dev), file (integration tests), or the internal bus topic eval-score-stream-v1 (prod). The publisher is behind a config gate (EVAL_SCORE_STREAM_ENABLED) and can be enabled per environment.

Safety/rollout: Backward compatible. Existing artifact formats remain valid; we only add new keys. The score stream publisher is disabled by default. Added guardrails to avoid emitting records when scorer output is missing/NaN. Added sampling controls for very large prompt sets: emit summary scores always; per-prompt scores remain only in artifacts, not in the stream.

Testing: Added unit tests for attribution propagation, JSON schema validation tests for stream records, and an end-to-end fixture that runs a tiny suite and asserts that emitted JSONL contains correct experiment_id/variant_id. Also added a compatibility test to ensure older consumers parsing metadata.json don’t break.

Follow-ups (not in this PR): (1) finalize Console-facing ‘quality’ metric mapping and thresholds (Applied ML + Product); (2) telemetry aggregation jobs (ENG-9027) to roll up score stream by {experiment_id, variant_id}; (3) documentation updates for customers/internal operators on how evals attach to experiments.

Risk notes: Cardinality is controlled by keeping the stream to a small dimension set; do not add raw prompt text or user identifiers. stickiness_key_hash is salted and truncated to prevent correlation across customers while allowing debugging for a single run.

source.title: Route-level A/B evals for quantization profiles (toggle + comparative metrics)
source.author: Olivia Grant
source.release_notes: Eval harness now supports route-level quantization profile A/B runs by toggling quantization settings via control plane, collecting comparative metrics, and producing a gating report suitable for rollout decisions.
source.body:
## Summary
Adds first-class support in eval-harness for running route-level A/B evaluations of the Quantization Profile Library.

This PR introduces:
- A route settings toggler that can set `quantization_profile_ref` for a route (and restore it) to compare baseline vs quantized behavior.
- A/B runner wiring that keeps prompts and sampling stable and attributes results to (a) requested profile ref and (b) observed applied profile (from response metadata/log annotations when available).
- Comparative metric aggregation and a single `gating_report.json` artifact with pass/fail and “why” fields that map to the quantization regression thresholds.

## Motivation
As part of the Quantization Profile Library rollout, we need deterministic, auditable route-level evaluation that can gate publication and canary enablement. Offline prompt-set evals are necessary but insufficient: route-level enablement introduces compatibility fallback behavior and runtime selection logic that must be validated end-to-end.

## Design highlights
- **Toggling model**: For a given `route_id`, the harness performs:
  1) Read current settings -> persist as `restore_token`.
  2) Apply baseline settings (`quantization_profile_ref=default`).
  3) Run A arm.
  4) Apply candidate profile ref (`quantization_profile_ref=<ref>`).
  5) Run B arm.
  6) Restore original settings even on error (best-effort + retries).
- **Determinism**:
  - A/B arms share the same prompt sample, seed, and request parameters.
  - Requests include `x-eval-run-id` and `x-eval-arm` headers.
- **Observed vs intended mode**:
  - The harness records `quant_profile_id` when returned in response metadata (or via optional trace export). If the runtime falls back, results are still captured but flagged and included in `fallback_rate`.
- **Guardrails**:
  - Hard fail if `fallback_rate` exceeds configured threshold (default 1%) or if compatibility errors exceed threshold.
  - Aggregates quality deltas and compares against policy thresholds (configured via `thresholds.yaml` aligned with the quantization regression policy).

## Outputs
Artifacts produced per run:
- `results_arm_a.jsonl` / `results_arm_b.jsonl` (per-prompt records)
- `metrics_summary.json` (arm metrics + deltas)
- `gating_report.json` (pass/fail + reasons + key deltas)
- `route_settings_audit.json` (before/after/restore plus timestamps and actor)

Example gating report excerpt:
- status: FAIL
- reason: "format_compliance_delta=-1.8pp exceeds threshold=-1.0pp"
- fallback_rate: 3.4%

## Backwards compatibility
No change to existing eval flows unless `--route-id` and `--quant-profile-ref` options are used (or `route_ab` config stanza is present). Existing prompt-set evals remain unchanged.

## Notes / follow-ups
- This PR assumes control plane endpoint availability in environments where route-level toggling is permitted. If RBAC denies the change, the run will fail with an actionable message and suggested workaround (run with a dedicated eval route).
- A future PR may add first-class integration to pull the compatibility matrix and preflight before toggling (to reduce noisy failures).
commits: Add control-plane client + auth plumbing for route settings
Implement QuantProfileRouteToggler with restore-on-failure semantics
Wire A/B runner and comparative aggregation into CLI entrypoint
Add gating report generator aligned with regression thresholds
Add unit tests with mocked control plane + golden artifact tests
Docs: add route-level quant profile A/B usage examples
conversation: Jonas Weber (2025-03-04): Can we ensure the sample prompts are identical between arms even if one arm hits transient 5xx and retries?
Olivia Grant (2025-03-04): Yes—sampling happens once up-front and is persisted to `sample_manifest.json`. Retries do not re-sample; they only re-issue the same request payload + prompt id.
Sana Farid (2025-03-05): Please add confidence intervals / significance where possible, otherwise gating may be flaky on small n.

Olivia Grant (2025-03-05): Added optional bootstrap CIs for key metrics when `--ci-bootstrap` is enabled; gating compares both raw delta and CI bound when present. Default remains deterministic delta-only for now per playbook.
Selene Huang (2025-03-05): We need a hard fail on fallback_rate; otherwise quality deltas may look fine while the route is silently running baseline.
Olivia Grant (2025-03-05): Implemented `--max-fallback-rate` (default 0.01) and included fallback reasons breakdown in the gating report.
Ethan Park (2025-03-06): Route settings restore must happen even when the process is SIGINTed. Did you add signal handling?
Olivia Grant (2025-03-06): Added SIGINT/SIGTERM handler to attempt restore with a 10s budget and log the restore outcome. Also writes `route_settings_audit.json` for postmortem/debug.

source.title: Introduce randomized counterfactual probe and soft-fail CI adapter
source.author: Ava Chen
source.description: Adds a randomized counterfactual probe to the eval-harness and a soft-fail CI adapter that surfaces regressions without hard-blocking merges unless they exceed configurable statistical thresholds. Motivation: many regressions are brittle to minor prompt edits; counterfactual probing produces small, semantically preserving edits and measures sensitivity. Design: probe generator implements configurable perturbation strategies (entity swap, tense shift, negation flip, punctuation perturbation) with deterministic seeding for reproducibility. The CI adapter runs a small batched probe set against the PR head, computes a bootstrap-based p-value and effect-size estimate, and returns either OK, WARN, or FAIL based on thresholds. WARN is marked as a soft-failure that appears prominently in PR checks and in the console, encouraging fixes without stopping fast-moving teams. Includes CLI flags --counterfactual-seed, --cf-soft-fail-threshold, --cf-min-samples and an autoscaling backoff that reduces probe size when CI concurrency is high. Ties into existing eval telemetry to record sensitivity_score per-prompt and cohort, enabling later alerting and triage. Backwards compatibility: the adapter is opt-in via .eval-config or pipeline flag and defaults to disabled for release branches. Risk: false positives for very small cohorts are mitigated by bootstrap confidence intervals and a minimum-sample guard. Linked work: ENG-4217 for probe design and ENG-4218 for CI adapter integration. Usage: run `eh run --probe counterfactual --seed 1234` locally or rely on the GitHub workflow `counterfactual-smoke.yml` which is added in this PR.
source.commits_summary: 5 commits; initial probe implementation and unit tests; CI adapter, workflow and docs; follow-up lint fixes and added deterministic seeding; updated telemetry tags and release note entry
review_comments: Diego Moreno: Nice approach; can you add an end-to-end test that runs the full adapter against a tiny local model?
Ava Chen: Added test_soft_fail_adapter.py which spins up the local mock and verifies warn/fail transitions under controlled seeds.
Priya Singh: Please ensure the default thresholds are conservative to avoid noisy CI. Consider a phased rollout.
Ava Chen: Defaults set to p<0.01 for hard-fail, p<0.1 with effect-size>0.05 for warn; added .eval-config opt-in and rollout notes in docs.
Ralph Becker: Flake8 complained about unused import in ci_adapters; saw and fixed in follow-up commit.
Priya Singh: Approved after tests and documentation updates.
CI Bot: unit-tests failed on first run (flake8 and a transient networking error), rerun passed.
source.merge_outcome: merged after approvals and green CI; squash merged to main; release note added
source.release_notes: Add counterfactual probing and soft-fail CI adapter: opt-in eval check that flags sensitivity regressions using randomized counterfactual edits. Defaults are conservative; enable via .eval-config. See docs/eval/counterfactual-probes.md for usage and tuning.
source.notes: This PR is intentionally conservative: probe generation is deterministic with seed, adapter is opt-in, and thresholds are tuned to reduce false positives. It enables later automation to pin problematic prompts into a quarantine cohort for targeted fixes.

source.title: introduce-adaptive-fidelity-curator-and-compact-failure-summarizer-ci-hook
source.author: Ethan Kim
source.release_notes: Add Adaptive Fidelity Curator CI hook that dynamically reweights prompt-set items based on short-term fidelity drift and a Compact Failure Summarizer that generates actionable regression scopes. Backfill job and docs included.
source.description: Motivation: Our existing CI smokechecks treat every prompt in a prompt-set equally which leads to noisy failures when a small subset of high-variance prompts trigger alerts. This PR introduces two coordinated pieces: (1) Adaptive Fidelity Curator: a CI hook that assigns per-prompt fidelity weights using an exponential-moving-average of recent evaluation scores and token-level stability signals, and exposes a soft-fail gating policy that scales alert severity by weighted SLO violations; (2) Compact Failure Summarizer: a post-failure analyzer that clusters failing examples by semantic/diff similarity, extracts exemplars, and produces a one-page failure summary with suggested roll-back scope (prompt IDs or prompt-cohort) for on-call triage. Changes in this PR are intended to reduce noisy alerts, prioritize high-impact regressions, and provide engineers with an immediate, digestible failure digest. Checklist: - [x] Implement Adaptive Fidelity Curator hook with EWMA weighting and cohort windowing - [x] Implement Compact Failure Summarizer using hierarchical clustering + diff compression - [x] Unit tests for weighting, gating, and summarizer outputs - [x] Add e2e smoke CI job wiring + sample prompt-set - [x] Update docs/ci_hooks.md and examples - [x] Add backfill job to compute initial fidelity baselines for existing prompt-sets - [x] Performance benchmarks for hook overhead (avg + p95 latency) Design notes: The curator maintains a sliding window of the last N CI runs per prompt (configurable; default N=30). For each prompt we compute a fidelity score f in [0,1] combining: normalized metric delta (metric-specific, e.g., exact-match/ROUGE) and token-stability score (1 - normalized edit distance across model outputs). We then compute an EWMA: w_t = alpha * f_t + (1-alpha) * w_{t-1}, alpha configurable per workspace. Per-prompt weights are normalized across the prompt-set and used to compute a weighted SLO breach score. Gate policy supports: hard-fail on weighted breach > T_hard, soft-fail (notify but don't block) on T_soft < weighted breach < T_hard, and auto-escalation if breach persists for M consecutive runs. Compact Failure Summarizer works after a CI failure: it vectorizes failing outputs using the eval-harness sentence-embedder (existing infra), clusters with average-linkage, selects top-k exemplars per cluster (k configurable), and produces a markdown summary with cluster descriptions, representative diffs, frequency, and a suggested scope for rollback. Small on-disk summarizer sample (embedded in tests) compresses diffs with a 3-line context window to keep summaries brief. Backfill: a job to run the curator against historical eval traces to seed initial weights. It runs as a separate scheduled job and writes fidelity baselines to the prompt-set metadata store. Safety and opt-outs: hooks are opt-in per-repo/prompt-set and respect existing ignore-lists and label overrides. Notably this PR does not change any default hard-blocking behavior unless the workspace enables weighted gating. Related work: ENG-4219 (fidelity-weighting design doc), ENG-4302 (CI alert noise audit). title: introduce-adaptive-fidelity-curator-and-compact-failure-summarizer-ci-hook
preview: Motivation: Our existing CI smokechecks treat every prompt in a prompt-set equally which leads to noisy failures when a small subset of high-variance prompts trigger alerts. This PR introduces two coordinated pieces: (1) Adaptive Fidelity Curator: a CI hook that assigns per-prompt fidelity weights using an exponential-moving-average of recent evaluation scores and token-level stability signals, and exposes a soft-fail gating policy that scales alert severity by weighted SLO violations; (2) Compact Failure Summarizer: a post-failure analyzer that clusters failing examples by semantic/diff similarity, extracts exemplars, and produces a one-page failure summary with suggested roll-back scope (prompt IDs or prompt-cohort) for on-call triage. Changes in this PR are intended to reduce noisy alerts, prioritize high-impact regressions, and provide engineers with an immediate, digestible failure digest. Checklist: - [x] Implement Adaptive Fidelity Curator hook with EWMA weighting and cohort windowing - [x] Implement Compact Failure Summarizer using hierarchical clustering + diff compression - [x] Unit tests for weighting, gating, and summarizer outputs - [x] Add e2e smoke CI job wiring + sample prompt-set - [x] Update docs/ci_hooks.md and examples - [x] Add backfill job to compute initial fidelity baselines for existing prompt-sets - [x] Performance benchmarks for hook overhead (avg + p95 latency) Design notes: The curator maintains a sliding window of the last N CI runs per prompt (configurable; default N=30). For each prompt we compute a fidelity score f in [0,1] combining: normalized metric delta (metric-specific, e.g., exact-match/ROUGE) and token-stability score (1 - normalized edit distance across model outputs). We then compute an EWMA: w_t = alpha * f_t + (1-alpha) * w_{t-1}, alpha configurable per workspace. Per-prompt weights are normalized across the prompt-set and used to compute a weighted SLO breach score. Gate policy supports: hard-fail on weighted breach > T_hard, soft-fail (notify but don't block) on T_soft < weighted breach < T_hard, and auto-escalation if breach persists for M consecutive runs. Compact Failure Summarizer works after a CI failure: it vectorizes failing outputs using the eval-harness sentence-embedder (existing infra), clusters with average-linkage, selects top-k exemplars per cluster (k configurable), and produces a markdown summary with cluster descriptions, representative diffs, frequency, and a suggested scope for rollback. Small on-disk summarizer sample (embedded in tests) compresses diffs with a 3-line context window to keep summaries brief. Backfill: a job to run the curator against historical eval traces to seed initial weights. It runs as a separate scheduled job and writes fidelity baselines to the prompt-set metadata store. Safety and opt-outs: hooks are opt-in per-repo/prompt-set and respect existing ignore-lists and label overrides. Notably this PR does not change any default hard-blocking behavior unless the workspace enables weighted gating. Related work: ENG-4219 (fidelity-weighting design doc), ENG-4302 (CI alert noise audit). source.title: Add bounded-confidence replay CI hook and progressive quarantine
source.author: Ava Chen
source.release_notes: Adds a bounded-confidence replay CI hook that adaptively replays failing prompt cohorts and a progressive quarantine policy. Non-breaking; feature-flagged. Includes metrics and dashboards.
source.description: Motivation: intermittent prompt-set regressions commonly triggered by sampling noise result in noisy CI failures and unnecessary quarantines. This change introduces a bounded-confidence replay CI hook that adaptively replays failing cohorts until a statistically sound confidence interval for the failure rate is obtained (Wilson score interval) or until a configured cap is reached. The hook also integrates a progressive-quarantine policy that transitions prompt cohorts through soft->hard quarantine states based on posterior confidence rather than raw failure counts. Design: ci/hooks/bounded_confidence_replay.py implements the replay loop, statistics and retry backoff; ci/policies/progressive_quarantine.yaml contains policy knobs (min_samples, max_replays, target_ci_width, soft_threshold, hard_threshold, decay). We expose a CLI toggle (--ci-bounded-replay) and a feature flag in CI config so rollout can be staged. Observability: replay-run provenance tags, counters for replays, per-cohort confidence width time-series, and a minimal Grafana dashboard are included. Testing: unit tests for statistical logic, integration test that simulates flaky prompts with seeded variability, and a GH Actions smoke matrix entry. Rollout plan: default disabled on main, enabled for eval-infra and canary repos for two weeks, then enabled org-wide if no regression signals. Checklist: [x] implementation + unit tests [x] integration smoke [x] metrics + dashboard [x] docs + runbook [x] Linear links added (ENG-2042).

b4e7c2d: add unit tests, integration GH Action matrix and microbenchmarks for replay latency
c9d91f0: add docs, runbook, dashboards and feature-flag toggle; address reviewer feedback
review_thread: Marcus Lee: This looks promising. Please add tests for the low-sample 'max_replays' path and ensure the Wilson interval implementation handles edge cases (0/0 and 1/1) — also prefer explicit seeding in the integration smoke to avoid flakiness.
Ava Chen: Added unit tests for 0/0 and 1/1 edge cases and forced seeds in the smoke integration. Also added doc note about numerical stability and clamped priors.
Diego Alvarez: Concerned about the cost of replays at scale. Can we cap total extra token spend per CI run and report it to telemetry?
Ava Chen: Added a configurable budget cap (per-run token_budget) and telemetry counters. Microbenchmarks included; default cap set conservatively in the policy file.
Samira Patel: Code looks clean, tests are comprehensive. Approving once CI green and one devops sanity check on the GH Action job timeouts.
CI Bot: All checks passed after rerun. Merge queued.

source.title: Introduce stochastic-consensus eval oracle and CI bridge for instability-aware gating
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.description: Motivation: We see intermittent flakiness in prompt-set regressions that traditional deterministic smoke checks miss. This PR adds a stochastic consensus evaluator that runs multiple randomized perturbations/seed variations per prompt, computes a consensus distribution over binary/metric-level outcomes, and exposes a probabilistic gate for CI. The goal is to reduce false positives (single-seed noise) while surfacing true instability and persistent regressions; it also provides quarantine suggestions for cohorts with high variance. What this implements: 1) eval_harness/oracles/stochastic_consensus.py - core evaluator implementing ensemble of perturbations, aggregation functions (consensus rate, trimmed mean, bootstrap CI), and an optional seed scheduler; 2) eval_harness/ci/consensus_ci_adapter.py - adapter that converts evaluator results into CI-friendly status with configurable thresholds for consistency_rate and p_value; 3) test-suite updates: tests/unit/test_stochastic_consensus.py + a deterministic seeding harness for CI replay; 4) docs: docs/eval/stochastic_consensus.md with algorithm notes and recommended thresholds; 5) examples: examples/prompt_sets/flaky_cohort.yml and a demo notebook converted to an integration test runner; 6) CircleCI integration hook: .circleci/consensus_gate.yml to run the adapter as a post-eval gate and annotate PRs. Backwards compatibility: the new oracle is opt-in via eval config (consensus: {enabled:true}) so existing pipelines are unaffected by default. Migration: a short guide in the docs shows mapping from single-run smoke gates to consensus gates and how to tune parameters. Checklist: [x] unit tests [x] integration smoke-run [x] docs [x] Linear links [x] added deterministic-replay mode that CI can use for reruns. Runtime considerations: default sample_count=15 and perturbations are lightweight (token-level suffix/noise paraphrases). We include an escape hatch to run in 'fast' mode (sample_count=5) for PRs with time constraints. Risk and mitigation: adds nondeterminism but provides deterministic-seed override and deterministic replay; thresholds default conservatively to avoid unnecessary failures. Next steps: add visualization hooks in Console and surface cohort quarantine recommendations to the rollout tool. See related work ENG-4521 and incident notes SUP-1182 for background.

Risk and mitigation: adds nondeterminism but provides deterministic-seed override and deterministic replay; thresholds default conservatively to avoid unnecessary failures. Next steps: add visualization hooks in Console and surface cohort quarantine recommendations to the rollout tool. See related work ENG-4521 and incident notes SUP-1182 for background.
text: Motivation: We see intermittent flakiness in prompt-set regressions that traditional deterministic smoke checks miss. This PR adds a stochastic consensus evaluator that runs multiple randomized perturbations/seed variations per prompt, computes a consensus distribution over binary/metric-level outcomes, and exposes a probabilistic gate for CI. The goal is to reduce false positives (single-seed noise) while surfacing true instability and persistent regressions; it also provides quarantine suggestions for cohorts with high variance. What this implements: 1) eval_harness/oracles/stochastic_consensus.py - core evaluator implementing ensemble of perturbations, aggregation functions (consensus rate, trimmed mean, bootstrap CI), and an optional seed scheduler; 2) eval_harness/ci/consensus_ci_adapter.py - adapter that converts evaluator results into CI-friendly status with configurable thresholds for consistency_rate and p_value; 3) test-suite updates: tests/unit/test_stochastic_consensus.py + a deterministic seeding harness for CI replay; 4) docs: docs/eval/stochastic_consensus.md with algorithm notes and recommended thresholds; 5) examples: examples/prompt_sets/flaky_cohort.yml and a demo notebook converted to an integration test runner; 6) CircleCI integration hook: .circleci/consensus_gate.yml to run the adapter as a post-eval gate and annotate PRs. source.title: Add multi-turn coherence smokehook and turnwise replay adapter
source.author: Priya Natarajan
source.description:
Motivation: A growing class of regressions only surface in multi-turn scenarios (turn-to-turn contradiction, context leakage, or degeneration across turns). Existing single-turn smokechecks miss these. This PR adds a multi-turn coherence smokehook and a turnwise replay adapter to detect and gate regressions that manifest across conversational turns.

What this PR introduces: 1) A coherence metric that combines (a) cross-turn semantic consistency (embedding-based similarity between prior assistant turns and subsequent responses) and (b) next-turn prediction stability (model likelihoods / token-level divergence when conditioned on different plausible prior paraphrases). 2) A smokehook that runs a compact multi-turn golden set on every canary and nightly CI run; the hook returns softfail/quarantine when drift exceeds thresholds. 3) A turnwise replay adapter which can: (i) expand failing examples into targeted replay samples (randomized paraphrases and alternate turn orders), (ii) tag failures with per-turn blame hints for faster triage. 4) Minimal UX: a small docs page and a local runner for debug.

Key goals: - catch regressions that affect conversation-level quality early - provide per-turn explanations so engineers can triage which turn(s) caused coherence loss - integrate with existing CI gating so teams can opt into hard vs soft failures

Checklist: - [x] Implement coherence metric and scoring shim - [x] Add multi-turn golden promptset (20 diverse dialogs across styles) - [x] Smokehook + CI job + YAML config - [x] Unit + lightweight integration tests - [x] Docs and runbook section - [x] Add telemetry tags for failure categories

Testing notes: The golden set is intentionally compact (~20 dialogs, 2-6 turns each) to keep runtime small in CI while retaining signal for common regression modes. Local runner supports --paraphrase N to exercise stability under input variation.

Rollout plan: Enable as soft-fail for 2 weeks on main branch runs; if signal is actionable reduce false positives then promote to hard gate for critical release branches. Linked Linear tickets contain follow-ups for threshold tuning and additional golden dialogs.

Marcus Li: This looks promising; have we measured false positive rate vs single-turn SLOs?

source.title: Add sparse error annotator and CI autorepair hook for flaky prompt-sets
source.author: Ethan Park
source.release_notes: Adds a sparse error annotator to reduce false-positive prompt-set regressions and a CI autorepair hook that attempts lightweight canonicalization on low-confidence failures. Improves gating stability and reduces noisy interrupts to rollouts.
source.description: Summary: This PR introduces a lightweight 'sparse error annotator' and an accompanying CI autorepair hook aimed at reducing noisy, low-density failures in prompt-set regression checks. The annotator detects when a regression signal is concentrated in very few prompts or is likely caused by brittle input formatting; the autorepair hook runs a set of deterministic canonicalizers on flagged examples and re-executes the failing tests in CI. If the example passes post-repair the check is quarantined as a probable flake instead of failing the entire job. Motivation: We saw recurring alerts in canary / smoke evals where 1-2 prompts per prompt-set caused CI failures that were later determined to be formatting or tokenization edge-cases. These false positives interrupt rollouts and cause unnecessary investigations. This change reduces noisiness while preserving sensitivity to real regressions.
commits: Initial sparse_error_annotator implementation: heuristics + embedding neighborhood smoothing
CI autorepair hook: integration with existing eval CI runner and quarantine logic
Unit + integration tests for annotator, autorepair path, and gating hysteresis
Docs: runbook update and console note for quarantined prompt-sets
review_conversation: Marina Kovács: Nice ergonomics; can you add a short-memory cache to avoid reprocessing the same prompt across repeated CI retries?
Ethan Park: Added an LRU cache with 10k entries — caches are namespaced by run-id to avoid cross-job leakage (see annotator cache util).
Diego Alvarez: The autorepair list looks powerful; are we confident it won't mask real regressions? Suggest adding a conservative confidence threshold and observability metrics.

Ethan Park: Agreed — added 'annotator_confidence' scoring (0-1) and only permit autorepair when confidence < 0.35 and flake_density < 0.05. Also emit metrics: eval.flake_rate, eval.repaired_pass_count, eval.annotator_confidence_bucket.
Priya Nair: Lint failed initially; please fix formatting and add a couple more unit tests covering punctuation-only repairs.
Ethan Park: Fixed lint issues and added tests. Re-ran CI — build+unit tests passed.
Marina Kovács: Approving after verifying metrics emitted in canary job. Diego: Approval granted.
Merge: Squash merge after CI green and approvals.
ci_checks: ci/unit-tests: passed (all annotator and autorepair integration tests)
ci/lint: failed on first push (style), fixed on second push
ci/eval-smoke: initially flagged 2 repaired examples; rerun post-autorepair marked as quarantined (no job failure)
notes_and_risks: Behavioral note: Autorepair runs only on low-confidence, low-density failures. High-density regressions still fail the gate.
Operational note: Quarantined prompt-sets are surfaced in the console under 'Eval -> Quarantines' and will be re-evaluated by scheduled backfills.

Risk: Over-aggressive canonicalization could hide real regressions; we intentionally kept repairs minimal (trim/normalize whitespace, punctuation normalization, simple date normalization) and require low annotator confidence before applying.
Mitigation: A monitoring alert triggers if repaired_pass_count / quarantine_count > 0.8 within a 24h window (indicates possible overfitting by repairs).
testing_and_validation: Added unit tests that exercise: high-density regression (should fail), single-example formatting flake (should be quarantined after autorepair), and confidence threshold boundaries.
Integration test in CI simulates a flake-class failure and validates quarantine + metric emission.
Ran a canary on internal prompt-sets for 48 hours — observed a 42% reduction in noisy CI failures for affected suites with no missed high-density regressions.
Manual review: product-quality team spot-checked 50 quarantined prompts; 44 were legitimate flakes (formatting/tokenization), 6 required human follow-up.
docs_and_runbook: docs/eval-runbooks/quarantine-and-autorepair.md added with operational steps for triage and unquarantine flow.
Console UI: new lightweight note on quarantined prompt-sets and auto-suggested canonicalization applied (visible to SRE/product reviewers).
Added a Linear checkpoint automation to raise a review ticket if quarantine age > 7 days for a prompt-set (linked to ENG-2187).
post_merge_actions: Deploy annotator library to staging eval-runner (automatic via pipeline)
Enable autorepair hook on master CI for canary projects only for first 72h, then roll to default if metrics OK
Create Linear task to monitor quarantine/repair metrics for 7 days (ENG-2114 follow-up)

source.title: Introduce soft-label probing and calibrated-expectation CI hook
source.author: Ava Chen
source.release_notes: Add a CI hook that probes prompt-sets with soft (probabilistic) labels to detect subtle semantic regressions; integrates with historical noise model to auto-calibrate tolerances and emits actionable failure reports.
source.commits: 3 commits: 1) add soft-label-prober core and tests, 2) wire prober into ci hooks and add metrics, 3) docs + changelog + minor lint fixes
source.description:
Context and motivation: Some regressions manifest as shifts in the model's probability mass over semantically equivalent answers rather than hard label changes. Existing binary pass/fail gates can be too brittle (false positives) or miss subtle quality drift. This PR adds a 'soft-label prober' and a calibrated-expectation CI hook that (a) probes prompt cohorts with synthetic soft labels (probability distributions over acceptable outputs), (b) compares aggregate likelihood mass assigned to expected semantic clusters, and (c) uses a noise-aware historical model to set dynamic tolerances so teams get fewer spurious CI failures while retaining sensitivity to true regressions.

Goals: 1) detect semantic drift that doesn't appear as single-answer mismatches; 2) reduce noisy CI failures by modeling historical variance per-cohort; 3) provide clear remediation artifacts (example worst-case prompts, delta distributions, suggested rollback windows).

Implementation summary:
- Core: eval_harness.probers.soft_label_prober.py implements probing loop, clustering helper (semantic buckets via embed+kmeans), and soft-label matcher that accepts distribution-level comparisons (KL, Earth Mover Distance).
- CI hook: infra/ci/hooks/calibrated_expectation_hook.py integrates with existing eval CI runner, computes cohort-level scores, consults the historical-stats cache to derive an adaptive tolerance (percentile-based) and emits a structured failure payload to the CI dashboard.
- Persistence: add historical noise ledger in eval_harness/storage/noise_ledger.sqlite (migration + lightweight updater) to capture per-cohort variance over time.
- Tests: unit tests for matcher heuristics and an integration smoke test that runs a synthetic prompt cohort against a mocked model returning controlled probability vectors.
- Docs: docs/eval/soft_label_prober.md describing configuration knobs, recommended cohort sizing, and troubleshooting.

Checklist:
- [x] Core prober implementation
- [x] CI hook wiring and gating behavior
- [x] Historical noise model and migration path
- [x] Unit + integration tests
- [x] Basic docs and changelog entry
- [x] Add telemetry and tracing spans for debugging

What changed (high-level file list):
- eval_harness/probers/soft_label_prober.py (new)
- eval_harness/probers/utils/semantic_bucket.py (new)
- infra/ci/hooks/calibrated_expectation_hook.py (new)
- eval_harness/storage/noise_ledger.py (new)
- tests/probers/test_soft_label_prober.py (new)
- tests/ci/test_calibrated_hook_integration.py (new)
- docs/eval/soft_label_prober.md (new)
- README and CHANGELOG updates (small edits)

Review conversation (highlights):
- Marcus Patel (reviewer): "Nice design. Can you clarify how you avoid small-sample overfitting when the ledger has < 50 historical runs?"
- Ava Chen (author): "Good point — I added a fallback smoothing that blends global cohort priors with observed ledger variance (lines 110-130 of noise_ledger.py). For very small histories we expand the tolerance to the 90th percentile of global cohort variance and annotate the CI failure as low-confidence."
- Leah Gordon (reviewer): "Can we make the default metric KL->EMD? KL is asymmetric and spikes easily when supports diverge."
- Ava Chen: "Switched primary metric to Earth Mover Distance (EMD) and left KL as an optional diagnostic — updated tests and docs."
- Ravi Singh (reviewer): "Telemetry looks good; add one more tag for 'cohort_size' on the failure event."
- Ava Chen: "Added cohort_size and ledger_depth tags to the telemetry envelope (see infra/telemetry/span_kv usage)."

CI history and reruns:
- Initial CI run (linux/unit) failed due to a flaky deterministic-seed in the semantic_bucket clustering test; failure mode: non-deterministic kmeans seeding. Action: fixed by adding a seeded kmeans helper and a retry-with-alternate-seed strategy in tests.
- Second run surfaced a lint failure (import ordering). Fixed.
- Final run: all checks pass (unit/integration/lint/security static checks).

Observability and dashboards:
- Added a lightweight REST endpoint in infra/ci/reports to surface the last 30 calibrated failures and show per-cohort tolerance bands.
- Added Grafana row: eval.soft_label_prober.failures with a breakdown by cohort and EMD delta.

Operational notes and configuration:
- New config block (eval.prober.soft_label) supports: sample_size (default 200), bucket_count (default 8), tolerance_percentile (default 95), min_ledger_depth (default 50), fallback_global_percentile (default 90).
- Hook respects repo-level overrides via eval.yaml in repo root (see docs).
- When a hook fails it uploads: worst_n_prompts.json, cohort_delta_distribution.csv, and a small replay bundle to the CI artifact store.

Migration/compatibility:
- Migration script eval_harness/storage/migrations/0002_add_noise_ledger.py included and idempotent.
- Existing CI gates are not changed; this hook is opt-in per-branch via eval.yaml.

Performance:
- Prober runs a single pass with batched embedding calls (reuses SDK embeddings cache) and samples 200 prompts by default; measured CI runtime impact ~+18s for a typical prompt set (acceptable for nightly and pre-merge pipelines).

Security and privacy:
- No plaintext customer data is written to the ledger; prompts are hashed with salt from KMS per-organization and only aggregate statistics are stored.

Tests and determinism:
- Added deterministic fixtures and a seeded clustering helper to reduce test flakiness (addresses earlier failure).
- Integration test uses a mocked model that returns explicit probability vectors to validate EMD thresholds and failure payload contents.

Follow-ups (post-merge):
- Add an autosuggest in the hook that recommends candidate rollback windows (ENG-2205).
- Evaluate calibrating on per-model family basis rather than global cohort (ENG-2221).
- Add optional human-in-the-loop review step for high-impact cohorts (product ask).

Example CI failure payload (brief): {"cohort":"password-reset-clarity","cohort_size":240,"ledger_depth":64,"emd_delta":0.27,"tolerance":0.15,"suggestion":"investigate top-3 prompts in worst_n_prompts.json"}

Merge rationale: After addressing metric choice, small-sample smoothing, and telemetry tags, reviewers approved. This hook reduces noisy false-positive CI failures in our eval pipelines while surfacing subtle semantic regressions that previously escaped hard-label checks.

Merge: squash-merged into main on 2024-11-20.

- [x] CI hook wiring and gating behavior
- [x] Historical noise model and migration path
- [x] Unit + integration tests
- [x] Basic docs and changelog entry
- [x] Add telemetry and tracing spans for debugging

What changed (high-level file list):
- eval_harness/probers/soft_label_prober.py (new)
- eval_harness/probers/utils/semantic_bucket.py (new)
- infra/ci/hooks/calibrated_expectation_hook.py (new)
- eval_harness/storage/noise_ledger.py (new)
- tests/probers/test_soft_label_prober.py (new)
- tests/ci/test_calibrated_hook_integration.py (new)
- docs/eval/soft_label_prober.md (new)
- README and CHANGELOG updates (small edits)

Review conversation (highlights):
- Marcus Patel (reviewer): "Nice design. Can you clarify how you avoid small-sample overfitting when the ledger has < 50 historical runs?"
- Ava Chen (author): "Good point — I added a fallback smoothing that blends global cohort priors with observed ledger variance (lines 110-130 of noise_ledger.py). For very small histories we expand the tolerance to the 90th percentile of global cohort variance and annotate the CI failure as low-confidence."
- Leah Gordon (reviewer): "Can we make the default metric KL->EMD? KL is asymmetric and spikes easily when supports diverge."
- Ava Chen: "Switched primary metric to Earth Mover Distance (EMD) and left KL as an optional diagnostic — updated tests and docs."
- Ravi Singh (reviewer): "Telemetry looks good; add one more tag for 'cohort_size' on the failure event."
- Ava Chen: "Added cohort_size and ledger_depth tags to the telemetry envelope (see infra/telemetry/span_kv usage)."

CI history and reruns:

- Second run surfaced a lint failure (import ordering). Fixed.

- New config block (eval.prober.soft_label) supports: sample_size (default 200), bucket_count (default 8), tolerance_percentile (default 95), min_ledger_depth (default 50), fallback_global_percentile (default 90).
- Hook respects repo-level overrides via eval.yaml in repo root (see docs).
- When a hook fails it uploads: worst_n_prompts.json, cohort_delta_distribution.csv, and a small replay bundle to the CI artifact store.

Migration/compatibility:
- Migration script eval_harness/storage/migrations/0002_add_noise_ledger.py included and idempotent.
- Existing CI gates are not changed; this hook is opt-in per-branch via eval.yaml.

Performance:
- Prober runs a single pass with batched embedding calls (reuses SDK embeddings cache) and samples 200 prompts by default; measured CI runtime impact ~+18s for a typical prompt set (acceptable for nightly and pre-merge pipelines).

Security and privacy:
